Description/Assumptions:
- Accessible by Super Admin only using email + password credentials.
- Account with Super Admin role must have been created in the system.
- If a valid session already exists => system skips the Login screen and navigates directly to the Dashboard.
- All connections must use HTTPS/SSL with TLS 1.2+.
- Maximum 5 consecutive failed login attempts before temporary lockout (15 minutes).
Fields/Components, Component Type, Requi:
Screen name: Login

- EZAxis logo - Display - Positioned top of page
- Email - Text field - Required - Placeholder: "Enter your email" - Max 100 characters - Must follow email format - Error indicator on empty/invalid field
- Password - Text field (masked) - Required - Placeholder: "Enter your password" - Min 8 characters - Error indicator on empty field
- Forgot Password? - Hyperlink - Positioned below password field - Navigates to Forgot Password screen (1.1.3)
- Login - Button - Primary - Full-width
User Flow:
Screen: Login (Super Admin)

Preconditions:
- No authentication required - this is the entry point for unauthenticated Super Admin.
- Super Admin account must already exist in the system with role = Super Admin.

User navigates to the Admin CMS URL.

1. System checks for an existing valid session cookie/token:
   1.1. If valid session exists => system does NOT render Login screen => navigates directly to Dashboard => flow ends.
   1.2. If no valid session => system renders Login screen.

2. Super Admin enters email and password.

3. Super Admin clicks [Login].

4. System validates all fields before querying the database:
   4.1. Email field empty => system does NOT query database => displays inline error below email field: "Email is required." => flow stops.
   4.2. Password field empty => system does NOT query database => displays inline error below password field: "Password is required." => flow stops.
   4.3. Email format invalid (missing @ or domain) => system does NOT query database => displays inline error: "Please enter a valid email address." => flow stops.

5. System checks failed login attempt count for this email address:
   5.1. If this email has >= 5 consecutive failed attempts within the last 15 minutes => system does NOT query database => does NOT attempt authentication => displays error: "Too many failed attempts. Please try again in 15 minutes." => flow stops.

6. System queries the database with the provided credentials:
   6.1. Email not found in system => system does NOT create session => does NOT reveal whether the email exists (security) => displays generic error: "Invalid email or password." => increments failed attempt counter for this email => flow stops.
   6.2. Email found but role != Super Admin => system does NOT create session => displays generic error: "Invalid email or password." => increments failed attempt counter => flow stops.
   6.3. Email found, role = Super Admin, but account Status = Inactive => system does NOT create session => displays error: "Your account is not active. Please contact the administrator." => does NOT increment failed attempt counter => flow stops.
   6.4. Email found, role = Super Admin, Status = Active, password does NOT match => system does NOT create session => displays error: "Invalid email or password." => increments failed attempt counter => flow stops.
   6.5. Email found, role = Super Admin, Status = Active, password matches => proceeds to step 7.

7. System creates an authenticated session:
   + Generates a secure session token.
   + Stores token server-side linked to Super Admin account.
   + Sets secure HTTP-only cookie on the client.
   + Resets the failed attempt counter for this email to 0.
   + Records last login timestamp on the account.

8. System navigates Super Admin to the Dashboard.

Edge cases:
- Network error during authentication => system displays error: "Unable to connect. Please check your connection and try again." => no session created => form remains filled.
- Session token creation fails server-side => system displays error: "Login failed due to a server error. Please try again." => no session created.
- Super Admin navigates to Login URL while already authenticated => system redirects to Dashboard without showing Login (step 1.1).
- Super Admin pastes email with leading/trailing spaces => system trims whitespace before validation.
   5.X. Account Status = Pending (invitation not yet accepted) => does NOT create session => error: "Your account is not yet activated. Please check your email for the invitation link."

Description/Assumptions:
- Accessible by authenticated Super Admin from any screen.
- Logout option available in the user header dropdown (top-right of all screens).
- A confirmation popup is shown before executing logout to prevent accidental sign-out.
- On logout: session token is immediately invalidated server-side.
Fields/Components, Component Type, Requi:
Component: User Header Dropdown (persistent - top-right on all screens)

- User avatar - Display - Circular avatar image or initials placeholder
- User name - Display - Full name
- Role badge - Display - Text: "ADMIN"
- Dropdown trigger - Icon button (chevron)
- Dropdown menu items:
  + Update Profile - Hyperlink - Navigates to Account Settings (1.3.5)
  + Change Password - Hyperlink - Navigates to Change Password screen
  + Sign out - Hyperlink - Triggers logout confirmation popup

Popup: Confirm Sign Out
- Message - Display - Text: "Are you sure you want to sign out?"
- Sign out - Button - Primary
- Cancel - Button - Secondary
User Flow:
Flow: Logout (Super Admin)

Preconditions:
- User must be authenticated as Super Admin.
- User header dropdown must be visible in the top-right corner of every authenticated screen.

1. Super Admin clicks the user avatar or chevron icon in the top-right header.

2. Dropdown menu expands with 3 options: Update Profile / Change Password / Sign out.

3. Super Admin clicks [Sign out].

4. System displays a confirmation popup:
   - Message: "Are you sure you want to sign out?"
   - Two buttons: [Sign out] (primary) and [Cancel] (secondary).

5a. Super Admin clicks [Sign out] (confirm):
   + System immediately invalidates the session token server-side.
   + System clears the session cookie from the browser.
   + System does NOT retain any session data or pre-fill credentials.
   + System navigates to Login screen (1.1.1).
   + No success toast needed - Login screen is visual confirmation of logout.

5b. Super Admin clicks [Cancel]:
   + Popup closes.
   + Super Admin remains on the current screen, fully authenticated.
   + No session change occurs.

Edge cases:
- Super Admin's session expires naturally (server-side timeout) while browsing => on next action that requires authentication, system redirects to Login screen with message: "Your session has expired. Please log in again."
- Super Admin attempts to navigate back (browser back button) after logout => system detects no valid session => renders Login screen instead of any authenticated page.
- Multiple browser tabs open: Super Admin logs out in one tab => other tabs detect session invalidation on next API call => redirect to Login screen automatically.
- Network error during logout => session cookie may still be present but server-side token is invalidated => system still navigates to Login. On next login attempt, the old cookie is discarded.

Description/Assumptions:
- Accessible by unauthenticated users from the Login screen.
- 2-screen flow: Screen 1 - Email input + OTP request; Screen 2 - OTP verification + new password entry.
- OTP is sent to the user's registered email via AWS SES / SendGrid.
- OTP validity: to be confirmed (assumed 10 minutes - flagged for BA review).
- After successful password reset => user is redirected to Login screen (1.1.1).
Fields/Components, Component Type, Requi:
Screen 1 - Enter Email

- Page title - Display - Text: "Forgot Password"
- Instruction text - Display - Text: "Enter your registered email address. We will send you a verification code."
- Email - Text field - Required - Placeholder: "Enter your email" - Max 100 characters - Must follow email format - Error indicator on empty/invalid
- Continue - Button - Primary - Full-width
- Back to Login - Hyperlink - Navigates back to Login (1.1.1)

Screen 2 - Enter OTP & New Password

- Page title - Display - Text: "Reset Password"
- Instruction text - Display - Text: "Enter the verification code sent to [email address] and set your new password."
- OTP - Text field - Required - Placeholder: "Enter verification code" - Max 6 characters - Numeric only - Error indicator on empty/invalid
- New Password - Text field (masked) - Required - Placeholder: "Enter new password" - Min 8 characters - Error indicator on empty
- Confirm Password - Text field (masked) - Required - Placeholder: "Re-enter new password" - Must match New Password - Error indicator on mismatch
- Resend Code - Hyperlink - Resends a new OTP to the same email
- Submit - Button - Primary - Full-width
User Flow:
Screen: Forgot Password (Super Admin)

Preconditions:
- No authentication required.
- User must have a registered Super Admin account with the provided email in the system.

--- Screen 1: Enter Email ---

1. Super Admin clicks [Forgot Password?] on the Login screen => navigates to Screen 1 (Enter Email).

2. Super Admin enters email address.

3. Super Admin clicks [Continue]:
   3.1. Email field empty => inline error: "Email is required." => flow stops.
   3.2. Email format invalid => inline error: "Please enter a valid email address." => flow stops.
   3.3. System queries the database for this email:
    + Email not found in system => system does NOT send OTP => displays error: "No account found with this email address." => flow stops.
    + Email found but account Status = Inactive => system does NOT send OTP => displays error: "Your account is not active. Please contact the administrator." => flow stops.
    + Email found, Status = Active => system sends OTP to email via AWS SES/SendGrid => navigates to Screen 2.

4. Super Admin clicks [Back to Login] => navigates to Login (1.1.1) without sending OTP.

--- Screen 2: Enter OTP & New Password ---

5. System displays Screen 2 showing the masked email address the OTP was sent to.

6. OTP delivery:
   + System sends 6-digit numeric OTP to the registered email.
   + OTP is valid for 10 minutes (pending confirmation - flagged for BA review).
   + Only 1 active OTP exists per account at a time. Requesting a new OTP invalidates the previous one.

7. Super Admin enters: OTP code, New Password, Confirm New Password.

8. Super Admin clicks [Reset Password]:
   8.1. OTP field empty => error: "Please enter the verification code." => flow stops.
   8.2. OTP contains non-numeric characters => error: "Verification code must be 6 digits." => flow stops.
   8.3. OTP is incorrect => error: "Invalid verification code. Please try again." => does NOT reset password => flow stops. (After 5 incorrect OTP attempts, OTP is invalidated and Super Admin must restart from Screen 1.)
   8.4. OTP is expired (> 10 min) => error: "This code has expired. Please request a new one." => OTP invalidated => system returns Super Admin to Screen 1.
   8.5. New Password field empty => error: "New password is required." => flow stops.
   8.6. New Password < 8 characters => error: "Password must be at least 8 characters." => flow stops.
   8.7. Confirm New Password does not match New Password => error: "Passwords do not match." => flow stops.
   8.8. All valid:
    + System updates the password in the database.
    + All active sessions for this account are immediately invalidated (forces re-login on all devices).
    + OTP is consumed and cannot be reused.
    + System navigates to Login screen (1.1.1).
    + Success toast on Login screen: "Password reset successfully. Please log in with your new password."

9. Super Admin clicks [Resend Code]:
   + Button is disabled for 60 seconds after each send (countdown timer shown).
   + After 60 seconds: Super Admin clicks [Resend Code] => system invalidates current OTP => generates new OTP => sends to same email => displays: "A new verification code has been sent."
   + Previous OTP is no longer valid.

10. Super Admin clicks [Back to Login] on Screen 2:
    + Navigates to Login (1.1.1).
    + OTP remains valid (not invalidated) until it expires naturally.

Edge cases:
- Email delivery fails (SES/SendGrid error) => system displays error: "Failed to send verification code. Please try again later." => does NOT navigate to Screen 2.
- Super Admin opens Forgot Password in a new tab while another reset is in progress => the latest OTP is always the valid one; earlier OTPs are invalidated.
- Super Admin attempts to reuse an already-consumed OTP => error: "Invalid verification code."
- Super Admin uses the same password as current password => system accepts it (no restriction unless business confirms otherwise - flagged for BA review).

Description/Assumptions:
- Accessible by all authenticated users from any screen.
- Supports English (EN) and Vietnamese (VI).
- Language switch applies immediately to all UI elements: menus, labels, buttons, system messages, and form placeholders.
- The selected language is persisted per user account across sessions.
- Feature is limited strictly to UI language switching - data content (asset names, descriptions, etc.) is not translated.
Fields/Components, Component Type, Requi:
Component: Language Switcher (persistent - in the top navigation bar on all screens)

- Language selector - Dropdown or toggle - Positioned in header navigation bar
- Values: EN (English) | VI (Vietnamese)
- Current language - Display - Shows selected language code (EN or VI)
- Language option - Selectable - Each option shows the language name in its own language (e.g., "English", "Ti?ng Vi?t")
User Flow:
Flow: Multi-Language Switch (Super Admin)

Preconditions:
- User must be authenticated (language switch available on all authenticated screens).
- Language switcher is visible in the top navigation bar on every authenticated screen.

1. Super Admin clicks the language selector in the navigation bar.

2. Dropdown/toggle renders with 2 options:
   + English (EN) - shown as "English"
   + Vietnamese (VI) - shown as "Ti?ng Vi?t"
   + Currently selected language is visually highlighted.

3. Super Admin selects a language (different from current):
   3.1. System immediately re-renders all UI text in the selected language:
    + All menu labels, button text, form placeholders, error messages, system notifications, and page titles switch to the new language.
    + No page reload required.
    + Data content (asset names, descriptions entered by users) is NOT translated - only UI strings change.
   3.2. System sends a request to save the language preference to the Super Admin's account in the database.
   3.3. System confirms save:
    + If save succeeds => preference persists across all future sessions on all devices.
    + If save fails (network error) => UI still displays in the newly selected language for the current session => system displays a non-blocking warning: "Failed to save language preference. The change will apply to this session only." => preference reverts to previous on next login.
   3.4. Selected language persists when Super Admin navigates between screens within the session.

4. Super Admin selects the same language already selected => no action taken, dropdown/toggle closes.

5. Super Admin closes the dropdown without selecting => no language change.

Edge cases:
- Super Admin switches language mid-form (e.g., while filling Create Asset form) => UI text re-renders in new language but field values entered by Super Admin are preserved.
- Super Admin switches language while a modal/popup is open => popup text also updates immediately.
- Super Admin has no language preference stored (first login) => system defaults to English (EN).
- Browser locale differs from system language setting => system respects the user's saved preference, not browser locale.

Description/Assumptions:
- Accessible to any invited user via a one-time invitation link (email).
- No authentication required — this is the entry point for newly invited users.
- Invitation link contains a signed token (expires in 72 hours).
- Account status is Pending until user completes this flow and sets a password.
- Applies to all roles: Admin, Building Manager, MSP Supervisor, MSP Technician.
- Link is single-use: once password is set, the token is consumed and the link expires.
Fields/Components, Component Type, Requi:
Screen: Set Password (Invitation)

- Welcome message: "You've been invited to EZAxis. Set your password to activate your account."
- Email address - Display (read-only, pre-filled from invitation)
- Full Name - Display (read-only, pre-filled from account)
- New Password - Password field - Required - Min 8 chars - Max 64 chars - Masked - Show/hide toggle
- Confirm Password - Password field - Required - Must match New Password - Masked - Show/hide toggle
- [Activate Account] - Button - Primary - Full-width
- Password strength indicator (Weak / Fair / Strong) shown below New Password field
User Flow:
Screen: Set Password (Invitation)

Preconditions:
- No authentication required.
- User accesses this screen via the invitation link in their email.
- Invitation token must be valid (not expired, not already used).

1. User opens invitation email => clicks [Accept Invitation] link.
2. System validates the invitation token:
   2.1. Token not found or malformed => system does NOT render Set Password screen => error page: "This invitation link is invalid. Please contact your administrator."
   2.2. Token already used (account already activated) => error page: "This invitation has already been accepted. Please log in." => [Go to Login] button.
   2.3. Token expired (> 72 hours) => error page: "This invitation link has expired. Please contact your administrator to resend the invitation." => no further action possible.
   2.4. Token valid => system renders Set Password screen pre-filled with user's email and full name (read-only).

3. User enters New Password and Confirm Password => clicks [Activate Account]:
   3.1. New Password empty => inline error: "Password is required."
   3.2. New Password < 8 characters => inline error: "Password must be at least 8 characters."
   3.3. New Password > 64 characters => inline error: "Password must not exceed 64 characters."
   3.4. Confirm Password does not match New Password => inline error: "Passwords do not match."
   3.5. Network unavailable => error toast: "No internet connection. Please try again."
   3.6. All valid:
    + System hashes and stores the password securely.
    + Invitation token consumed (permanently invalidated — cannot be reused).
    + Account status changed from Pending => Active.
    + System creates an authenticated session for the user.
    + System navigates user directly to their platform Dashboard (no need to log in again).
    + Success toast: "Account activated successfully. Welcome to EZAxis!"

4. If user closes browser before completing: token remains valid until expiry (72h). User can click the link again and complete setup.

Edge cases:
- User tries to use the same invitation link after successful activation => "This invitation has already been accepted. Please log in."
- User opens invitation link on mobile browser => same web flow renders responsively.
- Admin resends invitation before original expires => original token invalidated; new 72h token issued.
- User's email client mangles the link (line-break in URL) => link fails to open correctly; user must contact admin to resend.

Description/Assumptions:
- Accessible by Super Admin only.
- Entry point: navigate to Buildings in the left sidebar.
- Left panel: scrollable list of all buildings - supports search by name.
- Clicking a building in the sidebar loads that building's detail on the right.
- Each building row shows: Name, Status badge (Active/Inactive), floor count, address.
- [+ New Building] button in the top-right of the page header.
Fields/Components, Component Type, Requi:
Screen name: Buildings

Left sidebar (220px):
- Page header: "Buildings" + building count label
- Search - Text field - Placeholder: "Search..."
- Building list - Scrollable
  + Building name - Text - Bold - Clickable
  + Status badge - Active (green) / Inactive (amber)
  + Sub-label - Text - "[N] floors · [Address]"

Right detail panel (tabs):
- Building name - Display - Bold title
- Address + floor count + area count - Sub-label
- [Edit] - Button - Secondary
- [Delete] - Button - Danger (icon only)
- Tabs: Floors & Areas | As-Built Drawing | Info

Tab: Floors & Areas (default)
- [Add floor] - Button - Primary - top-right
- Floor accordion cards (per floor):
  + Floor name + area/unit count badge - collapsible header
  + Edit icon + Delete icon - in floor header
  + Area/unit tags (expanded) - pill tags: Area (green) / Unit (blue)
  + [+ Add] tag - dashed pill to add new area/unit inline

Tab: As-Built Drawing
- Empty state: Upload zone - PDF only - Max 50 MB - [Choose file] button
- Loaded state: PDF viewer with asset tag pins + [Edit tags] + [Download] + [Delete]

Tab: Info
- Grid: Building name / Status / Address / Total floors / Total areas/units

Empty state (no building selected):
- Right panel: "Select a building to view details"
User Flow:
Screen: Buildings (Super Admin)

Preconditions:
- Super Admin must be authenticated.
- Navigates to Buildings via the left sidebar navigation.

1. Super Admin clicks "Buildings" in the left sidebar.

2. System loads the Buildings screen:
   2.1. Left panel renders a scrollable list of all buildings in the system, sorted alphabetically by name.
   2.2. Each building row shows: Building Name (bold) / Status badge (Active green / Inactive amber) / Sub-label "[N] floors - [Address]".
   2.3. Building count label shown in left panel header (e.g., "4 buildings").
   2.4. If no buildings exist => left panel shows empty state: "No buildings. Click [+ New Building] to start." => right panel shows: "Select a building to view details."

3. Super Admin types in the Search field:
   3.1. System filters the building list in real time as Super Admin types.
   3.2. Filter is case-insensitive and matches partial names.
   3.3. If search yields no results => left panel shows: "No buildings match your search."
   3.4. Super Admin clears the Search field => full list restores.

4. Super Admin clicks a building row:
   4.1. Selected building highlighted in the left panel with a left border accent.
   4.2. Right panel loads that building's detail view with default tab = Floors & Areas.
   4.3. Right panel header shows: Building Name / [N] floors - [N] areas/units - Address.
   4.4. Action buttons in right panel header: [Edit] (Secondary) / trash icon (Danger).

5. Super Admin clicks the Floors & Areas tab (default):
   5.1. System renders floor accordion cards for the selected building.
   5.2. Each floor card shows: Floor Name / area/unit count badge / Edit pencil / Delete trash icon.
   5.3. Expanded floor card shows area/unit pill tags + dashed [+ Add] pill.
   5.4. If no floors exist => "No floors yet. Click [Add floor] to start."

6. Super Admin clicks the As-Built Drawing tab:
   6.1. If no drawing uploaded => upload zone shown (triggers 1.5.19).
   6.2. If drawing exists => PDF viewer rendered with asset tag pins.

7. Super Admin clicks the Info tab:
   7.1. Shows: Building Name / Status / Address / Total floors / Total areas/units in a grid layout.

8. Super Admin clicks a different building in the left panel:
   8.1. Right panel updates to show the newly selected building.
   8.2. Previously selected building loses highlight.

Edge cases:
- Super Admin navigates away and returns => last selected building is NOT remembered (resets to no selection).
- Very long building name => name truncated with ellipsis in the left panel row, full name shown in right panel header.
- Building with 0 floors => Floors & Areas tab shows empty state.
- System fails to load building list => error banner: "Failed to load buildings. Please refresh the page."

Description/Assumptions:
- Accessible by Super Admin only.
- Triggered by [+ New Building] button in page header.
- Creates a new building record. After creation, user immediately sets up Floors and Areas/Units within the same screen - no navigation required.
- The building appears instantly in the left sidebar after creation.
Fields/Components, Component Type, Requi:
Popup: New Building

- Building Name - Text field - Required - Max 100 characters - Must be unique - Error indicator on empty/duplicate
- Address - Text field - Optional - Max 255 characters
- Status - Dropdown - Required - Values: Active / Inactive - Default: Active
- Cancel - Button - Secondary
- Create - Button - Primary

After creation => popup closes => building auto-selected in sidebar => right panel opens on Floors & Areas tab => Super Admin continues adding Floors and Areas/Units using the [Add floor] and [+ Add] inline controls in the detail panel (see 1.2.1 Tab: Floors & Areas).
User Flow:
Flow: Create Building (Super Admin)

Preconditions:
- Super Admin authenticated, on Buildings screen.

--- Step 1: Create Building record ---

1. Super Admin clicks [+ New Building] in the page header.

2. System opens "New Building" popup modal.

3. Super Admin fills fields:
   - Building Name (required)
   - Address (optional)
   - Status (default: Active)

4. Super Admin clicks [Create]:
   4.1. Building Name empty => inline error on field: "Building name is required." => does NOT create => flow stops.
   4.2. Building Name exceeds 100 characters => inline error: "Building name must not exceed 100 characters." => flow stops.
   4.3. Building Name already exists (case-insensitive check) => inline error: "A building with this name already exists." => does NOT create => flow stops.
   4.4. Address exceeds 255 characters => inline error: "Address must not exceed 255 characters." => flow stops.
   4.5. All valid:
    + Building record created in database.
    + Building appears at the correct alphabetical position in the left panel list.
    + Building is auto-selected in the left panel (highlighted).
    + Right panel opens on Floors & Areas tab showing empty state: "No floors yet. Click [Add floor] to start."
    + Success toast: "Building created successfully."
    + Popup closes.

5. Super Admin clicks [Cancel]:
   + Popup closes.
   + No building created.
   + Previous selection in left panel is maintained.

--- Step 2: Add Floors (after building created) ---

6. Super Admin clicks [Add floor] button (top-right of right panel):

7. System opens inline prompt or small popup for Floor Name.

8. Super Admin enters Floor Name => clicks [Add]:
   8.1. Floor Name empty => error: "Floor name is required." => flow stops.
   8.2. Floor Name duplicate within this building => error: "A floor with this name already exists in this building." => flow stops.
   8.3. Valid => floor accordion card appears in Floors & Areas tab.
   8.4. Floor is auto-expanded showing empty area list with dashed [+ Add] pill.
   8.5. Building sub-label in left panel updates: "[N] floors".

9. Super Admin repeats step 6-8 for each additional floor (e.g., B2, B1, Ground Floor, F1, F2, Rooftop).

--- Step 3: Add Areas/Units per Floor ---

10. Super Admin clicks the dashed [+ Add] pill inside an expanded floor card.

11. System opens a small inline form or popup:
    - Type: Radio button - Area / Unit (required)
    - Name: Text field (required)

12. Super Admin selects type, enters name => confirms:
    12.1. Type not selected => error: "Please select Area or Unit." => flow stops.
    12.2. Name empty => error: "Name is required." => flow stops.
    12.3. Name duplicate within this floor => error: "A location with this name already exists on this floor." => flow stops.
    12.4. Valid => area/unit pill tag appears in the floor card.
    12.5. Building sub-label updates: "[N] floors - [N] areas/units".

13. Super Admin repeats step 10-12 for each area/unit on each floor.

Edge cases:
- Super Admin creates building then navigates away before adding floors => building is saved but has 0 floors (valid state).
- Super Admin adds floor then navigates away before adding areas => floor is saved with 0 areas (valid state).
- Network error during building creation => system displays: "Failed to create building. Please try again." => no partial record created.
- Super Admin attempts to create > 1 building with the same name across different capitalizations (e.g., "Landmark 81" vs "landmark 81") => system treats as duplicate (case-insensitive).

Description/Assumptions:
- Accessible by Super Admin only.
- Triggered by [Edit] button in the building detail header.
- Allows updating building name, address, and status.
- Floor and Area/Unit edits are done inline within the Floors & Areas tab - edit icons per floor/area.
Fields/Components, Component Type, Requi:
Popup: Edit Building

- Building Name - Text field - Required - Pre-filled - Max 100 characters
- Address - Text field - Optional - Pre-filled
- Status - Dropdown - Required - Pre-filled - Active / Inactive
- Cancel - Button - Secondary
- Save - Button - Primary

Inline edit for Floor (pencil icon in floor accordion header):
- Floor Name - Text field - Pre-filled - inline edit or small popup
- Save / Cancel

Inline edit for Area/Unit (pencil icon on area/unit tag):
- Name - Text field - Pre-filled
- Type - Radio - Area / Unit
- Save / Cancel
User Flow:
Flow: Edit Building, Floors, and Areas/Units (Super Admin)

Preconditions:
- Super Admin authenticated.
- A building must be selected in the left panel.

--- Edit Building Name/Address/Status ---

1. Super Admin clicks [Edit] button in the right panel header.

2. System opens "Edit Building" popup pre-filled with current values.

3. Super Admin modifies Building Name, Address, and/or Status => clicks [Save]:
   3.1. Building Name empty => error: "Building name is required." => does NOT save.
   3.2. Building Name exceeds 100 characters => error: "Building name must not exceed 100 characters." => does NOT save.
   3.3. Building Name duplicate (excluding current building) => error: "A building with this name already exists." => does NOT save.
   3.4. Valid:
    + Building record updated in database.
    + Left panel refreshes to show updated name.
    + Right panel header updates.
    + Success toast: "Building updated successfully."

4. Super Admin clicks [Cancel] => popup closes, no change.

--- Deactivate Building via Status field ---

5. If Super Admin sets Status = Inactive and clicks [Save]:
   5.1. System checks for linked active assets:
    + If active assets found => does NOT save => error: "Cannot deactivate - active assets are linked to this building."
   5.2. System checks for active Work Orders:
    + If active WOs found => does NOT save => error: "Cannot deactivate - active work orders are linked to this building."
   5.3. System checks for active Maintenance Plans:
    + If active plans found => does NOT save => error: "Cannot deactivate - active maintenance plans are linked to this building."
   5.4. No active dependencies => sets Status = Inactive => success: "Building updated successfully."
   5.5. Deactivated building: badge shows Inactive (amber) in left panel. Building no longer appears in dropdowns for creating new assets, WOs, or plans.

--- Edit Floor Name/Status ---

6. Super Admin clicks pencil icon on a floor accordion header.

Edge cases:
- Super Admin edits building name to the same name it already has => system accepts (no-duplicate self-check).
- Super Admin reactivates an Inactive building (Status back to Active) => no dependency check needed on reactivation.

Description/Assumptions:
- Accessible by Super Admin only.
- Triggered by the trash icon button in the building detail header.
- Hard-delete: permanently removes the building, all its floors, and all areas/units.
- Blocked if any active assets, work orders, or maintenance plans are linked.
- Requires confirmation popup with irreversible warning.
Fields/Components, Component Type, Requi:
Button: [Delete] - Danger icon button - in building detail header (trash icon)

Popup: Confirm Delete Building
- Warning message: "Delete [Building Name]? All floors and areas/units will also be permanently deleted. This cannot be undone."
- Delete - Button - Danger
- Cancel - Button - Secondary
User Flow:
Flow: Delete / Deactivate Building (Super Admin)

Preconditions:
- Super Admin authenticated.
- A building must be selected in the left panel.

--- Hard Delete ---

1. Super Admin clicks the trash icon button in the right panel header (building detail view).

2. System displays confirmation popup:
   - Warning: "Delete [Building Name]? All floors and areas/units will also be permanently deleted. This cannot be undone."
   - Two buttons: [Delete] (Danger) and [Cancel] (Secondary).

3. Super Admin clicks [Delete]:
   3.1. System checks for linked active assets:
    + If active assets exist => system does NOT delete => closes popup => displays error banner: "Cannot delete - [N] active asset(s) are linked to this building. Deactivate or reassign them first."
   3.2. System checks for active Work Orders (any status except Closed/Cancelled):
    + If active WOs exist => does NOT delete => error: "Cannot delete - [N] active work order(s) are linked to this building."
   3.3. System checks for active Maintenance Plans (Active or Pending status):
    + If active plans exist => does NOT delete => error: "Cannot delete - [N] active maintenance plan(s) are linked to this building."
   3.4. All dependency checks pass:
    + System deletes all Area/Unit records under all floors of this building.
    + System deletes all Floor records under this building.
    + System deletes the Building record.
    + System removes the building from the left panel list.
    + Right panel resets to empty state: "Select a building to view details."
    + Success toast: "Building deleted successfully."

4. Super Admin clicks [Cancel]:
   + Popup closes.
   + No change.
   + Building remains selected in the left panel.

--- Deactivate (via Edit Building) ---

5. Deactivation is performed via Edit Building (1.2.3) by setting Status = Inactive.
   (See 1.2.3 step 5 for full deactivation flow with dependency checks.)

--- Reactivate a Deactivated Building ---

6. Super Admin selects an Inactive building => clicks [Edit] => sets Status = Active => clicks [Save]:
   6.1. No dependency checks needed for reactivation.
   6.2. System sets Status = Active.
   6.3. Building badge shows Active (green) in left panel.
   6.4. Building becomes available again in dropdowns across the system.
   6.5. Success toast: "Building updated successfully."

Edge cases:
- Super Admin attempts to delete a building with only Closed/Cancelled WOs and no active assets => deletion is allowed (Closed/Cancelled WOs are terminal and do not block deletion).
- Super Admin attempts to delete the only building in the system => allowed (no system-level minimum building requirement).
- Network error during deletion => system displays: "Failed to delete building. Please try again." => no partial deletion occurs (atomic operation).
- Super Admin deletes a building that was being edited in another browser tab => the other tab receives a "Building not found" error on next action.
- Building has floors with no areas => deletion still proceeds; empty floors are deleted along with the building.

Description/Assumptions:

- Super Admin authenticated.

2. Super Admin fills required fields: Email / Full Name / Role.
3. Based on Role selected:
   + Admin => no assignment fields.

   5.4. Full Name empty => error: "Full name is required."
   5.5. Role not selected => error: "Please select a role."

Resend invitation (from User List or User Detail):

   + New invitation email sent.
   + Success toast: "Invitation resent to [email]."

- Deactivate icon not shown for Inactive users => system shows Reactivate option (via Edit) instead.

User Flow:
Flow: Edit User Group (Super Admin)

Preconditions:
- Super Admin authenticated.
- User Group must exist.

1. Super Admin clicks Edit icon on a group row (hover) OR clicks [Edit group] in the drawer footer.
2. System navigates to Edit User Group page, all fields pre-filled.

3. Super Admin modifies Group Info fields (Name, Description, Status).

4. Super Admin modifies Buildings in Scope:
   + Add buildings: click multi-select => select additional buildings => they appear as pill tags.
   + Remove buildings: click X on a building pill tag => building removed from scope immediately (pending save).

5. Super Admin modifies Asset Systems in Scope:
   + Same add/remove pattern as Buildings.

6. Super Admin modifies Members:
   + Add member: click [Add member] dropdown => shows users not yet in any group => select => member card appears.
   + Remove member: click X on a member card => confirmation prompt: "Remove [Name] from this group?" => confirm => member card removed (pending save).
   + If removed member is currently logged in => scope change takes effect on their next API call after save.

7. Super Admin clicks [Save]:
   7.1. Group Name empty => error: "Group name is required." => does NOT save.
   7.2. Group Name duplicate (excluding current group) => error: "A group with this name already exists." => does NOT save.
   7.3. All valid:
    + Group record updated.
    + Member additions: each added user linked to this group. If they were in another group, they are moved.
    + Member removals: each removed user unlinked from this group. They now have no group (no scope).
    + Building/System scope changes: all current members' effective scope updates immediately.
    + Success toast: "User group updated successfully."

8. Super Admin clicks [Cancel]:
   + Navigates back without saving.
   + No changes applied.

Edge cases:
- Super Admin removes all buildings from a group => group is saved with 0 buildings. Members effectively lose location scope.
- Super Admin removes a member who has an active WO in Started status => WO is NOT cancelled. Member retains their assignment on existing WOs. New WOs will not be assignable to this user via normal flow (no group = no scope in assignment dropdowns).
- Super Admin adds a member who is already in another group => system auto-moves them (removes from old group, adds to new group). Old group's member list is updated.
- Network error during save => system displays: "Failed to update group. Please try again." => no partial changes applied.

Description/Assumptions:
- Accessible by Super Admin only.
- Triggered by [Deactivate] button in drawer footer or via Edit (set Status = Inactive).
- Deactivated groups: members lose scope access immediately.
- Impact on active Work Orders when group deactivated: PENDING confirmation (flagged for BA review).
- Group data and members are preserved - can be reactivated.
Fields/Components, Component Type, Requi:
Button: [Deactivate] - Danger - in drawer footer

Popup: Confirm Deactivate User Group
- Message: "Deactivate [Group Name]? Members will immediately lose access to this group's scope."
- Confirm - Button - Danger
- Cancel - Button - Secondary
User Flow:
Flow: Deactivate / Reactivate User Group (Super Admin)

Preconditions:
- Super Admin authenticated.
- User Group must be in Active status to deactivate.

--- Deactivate ---

1. Super Admin opens the group drawer (click on group row).
2. Super Admin clicks [Deactivate] in the drawer footer.

3. System displays confirmation popup:
   - Message: "Deactivate [Group Name]? Members will immediately lose access to this group's scope."
   - Two buttons: [Confirm] (Danger) and [Cancel] (Secondary).

4. Super Admin clicks [Confirm]:
   4.1. System sets Group Status = Inactive.
   4.2. All members of this group immediately lose their scope access (buildings + asset systems).
   4.3. If any member is currently logged in => their next API request will return no scoped data. They will see empty lists for Assets, WOs, Inventory, etc.
   4.4. Active WOs assigned to members of this group: WOs remain in their current status. Members can still execute their assigned WOs if already started (WOs are not cancelled). New WOs cannot be assigned to them via normal flow.
   4.5. Group data and member list are preserved (not deleted).
   4.6. Group badge in table shows: Inactive (amber).
   4.7. Success toast: "User group deactivated."

5. Super Admin clicks [Cancel]:
   + Popup closes.
   + No change.

--- Reactivate ---

6. Super Admin clicks Edit icon on an Inactive group => Edit User Group page.
7. Super Admin sets Status = Active => clicks [Save]:
   7.1. No dependency checks needed for reactivation.
   7.2. Group Status set to Active.
   7.3. All members immediately regain their scope access.
   7.4. Group badge updates to Active (green).
   7.5. Success toast: "User group updated successfully."

Edge cases:
- Super Admin deactivates a group with 0 members => deactivation succeeds immediately (no member impact).
- Super Admin deactivates a group and then immediately reactivates it => members regain access without any data loss.
- Super Admin attempts to deactivate a group via Edit (set Status = Inactive) => same confirmation popup shown.
- Impact on active WOs is pending client confirmation - flagged for BA review.

User Flow:
Flow: Account Settings (Super Admin)

Preconditions:
- Super Admin authenticated.
- Accessible from any screen via user header dropdown.

--- Update Profile ---

1. Super Admin clicks user avatar/dropdown => clicks [Update Profile] => Account Settings page opens.

2. Super Admin modifies personal information:
   - Full Name (required, pre-filled)
   - Phone Number (optional, pre-filled)
   - Avatar (optional, pre-filled)

3. Super Admin clicks [Save]:
   3.1. Full Name empty => inline error: "Full name is required." => does NOT save.
   3.2. Avatar file type not JPG/PNG => error: "Only JPG and PNG files are accepted." => does NOT save.
   3.3. Avatar file > 2MB => error: "File size must not exceed 2 MB." => does NOT save.
   3.4. All valid => profile updated in database => avatar updates in header dropdown immediately => success toast: "Profile updated successfully."

4. Super Admin clicks [Cancel] => navigates back, no change.

--- Change Password ---

5. Super Admin clicks [Change Password] from the Account Settings page or from the header dropdown.

6. Super Admin enters:
   - Current Password (required, masked)
   - New Password (required, masked)
   - Confirm New Password (required, masked)

7. Super Admin clicks [Save]:
   7.1. Current Password field empty => error: "Current password is required." => does NOT save.
   7.2. Current Password incorrect => error: "Current password is incorrect." => does NOT save.
   7.3. New Password field empty => error: "New password is required." => does NOT save.
   7.4. New Password < 8 characters => error: "Password must be at least 8 characters." => does NOT save.
   7.5. Confirm New Password does not match New Password => error: "Passwords do not match." => does NOT save.
   7.6. All valid:
    + Password updated in database.
    + All other active sessions for this account are immediately invalidated (forces re-login on other devices).
    + Current session remains active (Super Admin stays logged in on the current device).
    + Success toast: "Password changed successfully."

8. Super Admin clicks [Cancel] => navigates back, no change.

Edge cases:
- Super Admin uploads a new avatar while on a slow connection => loading spinner shown during upload => progress indicator if possible.
- Super Admin sets the same password as the current password => system accepts it (no restriction unless business specifies otherwise - flagged for BA review).
- Network error during profile save => "Failed to update profile. Please try again." => no partial change.
- Network error during password change => "Failed to change password. Please try again." => password NOT changed.

Description/Assumptions:
- Accessible by Super Admin.
- System-wide overview across all buildings.
- Filter by Building and Date Range.
- Shows WO status trend, maintenance Gantt, KPI cards.
Fields/Components, Component Type, Requi:
Screen name: Dashboard
- Building - Dropdown - All Buildings + list - Default: All
- Date Range - Date range picker - Default: last 30 days
- KPI cards: Total WOs / Pending / In Progress / Completed / Closed
- WO Status Trend - Line chart - X: months - Lines: Todo / Completed / Closed
- WO Type filter - Dropdown - All / Maintenance / Ad-hoc / Service Request

User Flow:
Screen: Performance Dashboard (Super Admin)

Preconditions:
- Super Admin authenticated.
- Dashboard is the default landing screen after login.

1. System loads Dashboard with default filters on first render:
   + Building filter: All Buildings.
   + Date Range: last 30 days (pending client confirmation - flagged for BA review).
   + WO Type filter: All types.

2. System fetches and renders all dashboard sections simultaneously:
   2.1. KPI cards: Total WOs / Pending Approval / In Progress / Completed / Closed - each shows a count for the selected date range and building scope.
   2.2. WO Status Trend chart: line chart with 3 lines (Todo / Completed / Closed) across the date range. X-axis = time, Y-axis = WO count.
   2.3. Maintenance Progress Gantt: table with Asset System > Sub-system > Asset Type rows and month columns. Each cell shows round label + completion rate (e.g., R1: 75%).
   2.4. If any section fails to load => that section shows an inline error: "Failed to load [section name]. Click to retry."

3. Super Admin changes Building filter:
   3.1. Selects a specific building from dropdown => all KPI cards, Trend chart, and Gantt re-fetch and re-render scoped to that building.
   3.2. Selects "All Buildings" => data reloads for all buildings combined.
   3.3. Loading indicator shown during re-fetch.

4. Super Admin changes Date Range:
   4.1. Clicks the date range picker => selects From Date and To Date.
   4.2. If To Date < From Date => error: "End date must be after start date." => does NOT apply.
   4.3. If date range > 12 months => warning: "Large date ranges may take longer to load." => still allows.
   4.4. Valid range => all sections re-fetch and re-render.

5. Super Admin changes WO Type filter:
   5.1. Selects a type (Maintenance Scheduling / Ad-hoc Work / Service Request) => Trend chart and KPI cards update.
   5.2. Gantt chart is not affected by WO Type filter (Gantt is always Maintenance Scheduling only).

6. Super Admin hovers over a data point on the Trend chart:
   + Tooltip shows: date / Todo count / Completed count / Closed count for that point.

7. Super Admin clicks a KPI card:
   7.1. System navigates to Work Order List (1.8.3) pre-filtered to that status group.
   7.2. E.g., clicking "Pending Approval" navigates to WO List filtered to Pending status.

8. Super Admin hovers over a Gantt cell:
   + Tooltip shows: plan name / round number / completed WO count / total WO count / completion percentage.

9. Empty state:
   + If no WOs exist in the selected period/building => KPI cards show 0 / Trend chart shows flat lines / Gantt shows "No data for the selected period."

Edge cases:
- Dashboard loads but one API endpoint times out => affected section shows retry option; other sections render normally.
- Super Admin filters to a building with no maintenance plans => Gantt shows: "No maintenance plans for the selected building and period."
- Super Admin selects a future date range => KPI cards may show 0 or partial data (planned WOs not yet executed).
- Super Admin resizes browser window => dashboard layout adjusts responsively; charts re-render at new dimensions.

User Flow:
Screen: Maintenance Trend Report (Super Admin)

Preconditions:
- Super Admin authenticated.

1. Super Admin navigates to Dashboard => Maintenance Trend Report tab (or Reports section).

   + Building: All Buildings.

3. System fetches and renders 3 charts:

   Chart 1 - Failure Trends:
   + X-axis: time (by selected granularity).

   + X-axis: time.
   + Y-axis: count.
   + "Planned" line: number of WOs auto-generated from active Maintenance Plans in each period.

   + Same as Performance Dashboard trend chart.

User Flow:
Flow: Report Builder - Maintenance Progress (Super Admin)

Preconditions:
- Super Admin authenticated.

1. Super Admin navigates to Report Builder => Maintenance Progress tab (Tab 1).

2. System renders filter section:
   + Building dropdown (required) - default: All Buildings.
   + From Month picker (required) - default: 6 months ago.
   + To Month picker (required) - default: current month.
   + [Apply] button.

3. Super Admin selects filters and clicks [Apply]:

   3.2. From Month not selected => error: "Please select a start month." => does NOT fetch.

Edge cases:
- Super Admin changes filters after generating preview => previous preview clears => must click [Apply] again.
- Report file size is very large (many buildings, long date range) => system may paginate or split the download (handling TBC).

Description/Assumptions:
- Same full-page form as Create, pre-filled.
- Asset Code uniqueness check excludes current asset.
Fields/Components, Component Type, Requi: Page: Edit Asset - same layout as New Asset (1.5.14), all fields pre-filled.
User Flow:
Flow: Edit Asset (Super Admin)

Preconditions:
- Super Admin authenticated.

1. Super Admin clicks Edit icon on an asset row OR clicks [Edit] on Asset Detail page.
2. System navigates to Edit Asset page, all fields pre-filled with current values.

3. Super Admin modifies fields (all same fields as Create, except Asset Code uniqueness check excludes current asset).

4. Super Admin clicks [Save]:
   4.1. All same validation rules as Create (1.5.14).
   4.2. Asset Code duplicate excluding current asset => error: "This asset code already exists."
   4.3. All valid => asset record updated => success toast: "Asset updated successfully."

5. Super Admin clicks [Cancel] => navigates back without saving.

Edge cases:
- Super Admin changes Asset Type => the asset's checklist template changes for future WOs. Existing WOs in progress are NOT affected.
- Super Admin changes Building or Floor => asset location is updated. Existing WOs linked to this asset remain with original location reference in their records.
- Super Admin changes Status to Inactive => same as Deactivate flow (1.5.16), dependency checks apply.
- Network error => "Failed to update asset. Please try again."

Description/Assumptions:
- Deactivation (not hard-delete). Historical data preserved.
- Deactivated assets no longer available for new WOs or Maintenance Plans.
Fields/Components, Component Type, Requi:
Popup: Confirm Deactivate Asset
- Message: "Deactivate [Asset Name]? The asset record and history will be preserved."
- Confirm - Danger / Cancel
User Flow:
Flow: Deactivate Asset (Super Admin)

Preconditions:
- Super Admin authenticated.
- Asset must be in Active status.

1. Super Admin clicks Deactivate icon on an asset row OR clicks [Deactivate] on Asset Detail page.

2. System displays confirmation popup:
   - Message: "Deactivate [Asset Name]? The asset record and all history will be preserved."
   - Buttons: [Confirm] (Danger) / [Cancel] (Secondary).

3. Super Admin clicks [Confirm]:
   3.1. System checks for active WOs linked to this asset (Assigned or Started status):
    + If active WOs exist => does NOT deactivate => error: "Cannot deactivate - [N] active work order(s) are linked to this asset."
   3.2. System checks for active Maintenance Plans that include this asset in their Assets tab:
    + If active plans include this asset => does NOT deactivate => error: "Cannot deactivate - this asset is included in active maintenance plan(s)."
   3.3. All checks pass:
    + Asset Status set to Inactive.
    + Asset no longer available in dropdowns for new WO creation.
    + Asset no longer generates WOs from Maintenance Plans going forward.
    + Asset still appears in historical records (past WOs, maintenance history) with Inactive badge.
    + Success toast: "Asset deactivated successfully."

4. Super Admin clicks [Cancel] => popup closes, no change.

Reactivate:
5. Super Admin finds Inactive asset (filter by Status: Inactive in Asset List).
6. Super Admin opens Asset Detail => clicks [Activate]:
   + Confirmation popup.
   + Confirms => Status set to Active => asset returns to all dropdowns => success: "Asset activated successfully."

Edge cases:
- Deactivating an asset that has only Closed/Cancelled WOs => allowed (no active WOs).
- Deactivating an asset included in a Pending (not Active) Maintenance Plan => flagged for BA review.
- Network error => "Failed to deactivate asset. Please try again."

Description/Assumptions:
- Generates and downloads the QR code for a specific asset.
- QR code is auto-generated on asset creation and is permanent.
- Download format: PNG or PDF.
Fields/Components, Component Type, Requi:
Component: Download QR (from Asset List and Asset Detail)
- Download QR - Icon button (QR icon) on asset row / Button on Asset Detail
- Format: PNG / PDF (to be confirmed)
User Flow:
Flow: Download Asset QR Code (Super Admin)

Preconditions:
- Super Admin authenticated.
- Asset must exist and have a QR code generated (QR is auto-generated on asset creation).

1. Super Admin clicks Download QR icon on:
   + Asset List row (Actions column), OR
   + Asset Detail page ([Download QR] button).

2. System generates the QR code file:
   2.1. QR code encodes the asset's URL (deep link to Asset Detail page).
   2.2. File format: PNG (default) or PDF label format (pending client confirmation - flagged for BA review).
   2.3. File is named: "[Asset Code]_QR.[format]" (e.g., "FCU-001_QR.png").

3. File downloads to the user's browser download folder.

4. Success: no toast needed - file download is visual confirmation.

5. If QR generation fails:
   + Error toast: "Unable to generate QR code. Please try again."
   + No file downloaded.

Edge cases:
- Super Admin downloads QR for an Inactive asset => download still works (QR is permanent and linked to the asset record regardless of status).
- Super Admin scans the downloaded QR with a phone camera (not the app) => opens the asset detail URL in the browser. If the user is not authenticated, they are redirected to Login first.
- QR code URL format: pending confirmation of whether it links to the web portal or deep-links into the mobile app.

Description/Assumptions:
- Allows creating multiple assets via CSV file upload.
- Template downloadable. Valid rows inserted; invalid rows returned with error report.
- Each imported asset auto-gets a QR code.
Fields/Components, Component Type, Requi:
Screen name: Bulk Upload Asset
- Instruction text + Download Template - Button - Secondary
- File input - Accepts CSV only - Max 10 MB
- Upload - Button - Primary - Disabled until file selected

Import Result (after upload):
- Summary: "[N] assets imported. [M] rows rejected."
- Download Error Report - Button - Conditional (visible if M > 0)
- View Imported Assets - Hyperlink
User Flow:
Flow: Bulk Upload Asset via CSV (Super Admin)

Preconditions:
- Super Admin authenticated.
- Asset Systems, Sub-systems, Asset Types, Buildings, Floors must already exist (CSV data references these).

1. Super Admin navigates to Assets => Bulk Upload.

2. System renders upload screen:
   + [Download Template] button => downloads the CSV template with correct column headers.
   + File input (accepts CSV only, max 10 MB).
   + [Upload] button (disabled until file selected).

3. Super Admin clicks [Download Template]:
   + CSV template downloads with headers: Asset Name / Asset Code / Asset System / Asset Sub-system / Asset Type / Building / Floor / Area/Unit / Model / Serial Number / Brand / Purchase Date / Status.
   + First row contains headers; second row contains an example entry.

4. Super Admin fills the template, saves as CSV, then selects the file.

5. Super Admin clicks [Upload]:
   5.1. File is not CSV => error: "Only CSV files are accepted."
   5.2. File > 10MB => error: "File size must not exceed 10 MB."
   5.3. CSV cannot be parsed (corrupted format) => error: "Invalid file format. Please use the provided template."
   5.4. Valid file => system begins processing row by row.

6. System validates each row:
   + Required fields missing => row rejected with reason.
   + Asset Code duplicate (in system or within the same CSV file) => row rejected.
   + Asset System/Sub-system/Asset Type not found in system => row rejected.
   + Building/Floor not found => row rejected.
   + Invalid date format => row rejected.
   + Status value not "Active" or "Inactive" => row rejected.

7. After processing all rows:
   + Valid rows => Assets created in database, each with auto-generated QR code.
   + Invalid rows => collected into an error report.
   + System renders import summary: "[N] assets imported. [M] rows rejected."
   + If M > 0 => [Download Error Report] button appears => CSV with rejected rows + reason column.
   + [View Imported Assets] hyperlink => navigates to Asset List filtered to show only newly imported assets.

8. Success toast: "[N] assets imported successfully."

Edge cases:
- All rows valid => M = 0 => no error report generated.
- All rows invalid => N = 0 => warning: "No assets were imported. Please review the error report."
- CSV has 0 data rows (only headers) => warning: "The uploaded file contains no data."
- CSV has 1000+ rows => system processes asynchronously, shows progress indicator.
- Network error during upload => "Upload failed. Please check your connection and try again." => no partial import.
- Two admins upload at the same time with overlapping Asset Codes => first-in-wins; second upload will have those rows rejected as duplicate codes.

Description/Assumptions:
- Accessible by Super Admin and Building Manager (BM scoped to assigned buildings).
- PDF format only (MVP). Max 50MB.
- One drawing per building (per current assumption - pending Q5 confirmation).
- If drawing already exists => replace flow triggers.
Fields/Components, Component Type, Requi:
Screen name: Drawing Management (Assets => Drawing)

Empty state (no drawing):
- Building - Dropdown - Required
- Empty state message: "No drawing uploaded for this building."
- Upload Drawing - Button - Primary => opens file picker

File picker:
- Accepted: PDF only - Max 50 MB
User Flow:
Flow: Upload As-Built Drawing (Super Admin)

Preconditions:
- Super Admin authenticated.
- Building must exist.

1. Super Admin navigates to Assets => Drawing.
2. Super Admin selects a Building from the dropdown.

3. System checks if a drawing already exists for the selected building:
   3a. No drawing exists:
    + System shows empty state: "No drawing uploaded for [Building Name]."
    + [Upload Drawing] button shown.
   3b. Drawing already exists:
    + System shows existing drawing in PDF viewer.
    + [Replace Drawing] button shown with warning label.

4. Super Admin clicks [Upload Drawing] (new) or [Replace Drawing] (existing):
   + File picker opens (accepts PDF only).

5. If replacing: system shows confirmation popup BEFORE file picker:
   - Message: "Uploading a new drawing will replace the existing one and remove all asset tags. This cannot be undone."
   - Buttons: [Continue] / [Cancel].
   - Super Admin clicks [Cancel] => popup closes, no action.
   - Super Admin clicks [Continue] => file picker opens.

6. Super Admin selects a PDF file:
   6.1. File is not PDF => error: "Only PDF files are accepted."
   6.2. File > 50MB => error: "File size must not exceed 50 MB."
   6.3. Valid file => system uploads to cloud storage:
    + Progress indicator shown during upload.
    + Upload takes > 5 seconds => "Uploading drawing, please wait..."
    6.4. Upload succeeds:
    + PDF viewer renders the uploaded drawing.
    + If replacement: all previous asset tags are permanently removed.
    + Success toast (new): "Drawing uploaded successfully."
    + Success toast (replace): "Drawing replaced. All previous asset tags have been removed."
   6.5. Upload fails (network error or storage error):
    + Error: "Failed to upload drawing. Please try again."
    + Previous drawing (if any) is preserved (no partial replacement).

7. Super Admin clicks [Cancel] in file picker => no action, page unchanged.

Edge cases:
- Super Admin uploads a password-protected PDF => upload succeeds but PDF viewer cannot render it => error in viewer: "This PDF is password protected and cannot be displayed."
- Super Admin uploads a scanned PDF (no text layer) => upload succeeds; viewer renders it as image; asset tags can still be placed on coordinates.
- Building has no floors => drawing can still be uploaded (drawing is at building level, not floor level - pending client confirmation on drawing scope).

Description/Assumptions:
- Confirmed permissions:
  • Web view: Super Admin, Building Manager, MSP Supervisor
  • Mobile view (read-only): Building Manager, MSP Supervisor, MSP Technician
- PDF viewer with pan/zoom.
Fields/Components, Component Type, Requi:
Screen name: Drawing Management (with drawing loaded)
- Building - Dropdown
- PDF Viewer - Component (pdfjs-dist) - Pan and zoom supported
- File name + Upload date + Uploaded by - Display
- Download PDF - Button - Secondary
- Replace Drawing - Button - Secondary
- Delete Drawing - Button - Danger
User Flow:
Flow: View As-Built Drawing (Super Admin / BM / Supervisor)

Preconditions:
- User authenticated with appropriate role (Super Admin, Building Manager, or MSP Supervisor).
- A drawing must be uploaded for the selected building.

1. User navigates to Assets => Drawing.
2. User selects a Building from the dropdown (scoped by role: BM sees only assigned buildings; Supervisor sees only group buildings).

3. System checks if a drawing exists:
   3a. No drawing => empty state with upload option (if user has upload rights) or "No drawing available for this building."
   3b. Drawing exists => system renders PDF in PDF viewer component (pdfjs-dist).

4. User interactions with PDF viewer:
   + Pan: click and drag to move around the drawing.
   + Zoom: scroll wheel (desktop) / pinch gesture (touch) to zoom in/out.
   + Reset view: double-click to reset to default zoom.

5. Asset tag pins are visible on the drawing (read-only in view mode):
   + Pins are positioned at their saved (x, y) coordinates.
   + Different pin colors may indicate different asset system types (pending design confirmation).

6. User clicks/taps a tag pin:
   + Popup appears near the pin showing:
    - Asset Name
    - Asset Code
    - Asset Type
    - Asset System
    - Current Status badge (Active / Inactive)
    - Last Maintenance Date
    - [Link to Asset Detail] hyperlink => navigates to Asset Detail page.
    - [X] Close button.
   + Clicking outside popup => popup closes.

7. Additional controls (for users with upload rights):
   + [Download PDF] => downloads the drawing file.
   + [Replace Drawing] => triggers upload flow (1.5.19).
   + [Delete Drawing] => triggers delete flow (1.5.21).
   + [Edit Tags] => enters tag editing mode.

8. Read-only users (Supervisor, Technician on mobile):
   + Only see the drawing and pin popups.
   + No [Edit Tags], [Replace], or [Delete] controls shown.

Edge cases:
- PDF viewer fails to render (corrupt file, unsupported PDF version) => error: "Unable to display this drawing. Please try re-uploading the file."
- PDF is very large (100+ pages, large file size) => viewer loads first page first; remaining pages load progressively.
- User clicks a pin for a deactivated asset => popup still shown with Inactive badge.

Description/Assumptions:
- Accessible by Super Admin and Building Manager.
- Deleting a drawing also removes all asset tags placed on it.
- Irreversible - requires confirmation with warning.
Fields/Components, Component Type, Requi:
Popup: Confirm Delete Drawing
- Message: "Delete the drawing for [Building Name]? All asset tags will also be removed. This cannot be undone."
- Delete - Button - Danger
- Cancel - Button - Secondary
User Flow:
Flow: Delete As-Built Drawing (Super Admin / BM)

Preconditions:
- Super Admin or Building Manager authenticated.
- A drawing must be uploaded for the selected building.

1. User is on the Drawing screen with a drawing loaded.
2. User clicks [Delete Drawing] button.

3. System displays confirmation popup:
   - Message: "Delete the drawing for [Building Name]? All asset tags placed on this drawing will also be permanently removed. This cannot be undone."
   - Buttons: [Delete] (Danger) / [Cancel] (Secondary).

4. User clicks [Delete]:
   4.1. System permanently deletes the drawing file from cloud storage.
   4.2. System permanently removes all asset tag records associated with this drawing.
   4.3. Drawing viewer area resets to empty state: "No drawing uploaded for [Building Name]."
   4.4. [Upload Drawing] button appears.
   4.5. Success toast: "Drawing deleted successfully."

5. User clicks [Cancel] => popup closes, drawing and tags remain unchanged.

Edge cases:
- Drawing has 0 asset tags => deletion proceeds without the tag-removal warning being particularly relevant (but warning is still shown for consistency).
- Network error during deletion => "Failed to delete drawing. Please try again." => no partial deletion (drawing file and tags preserved).
- User deletes drawing and then immediately uploads a new one => the new drawing starts with 0 tags.

Description/Assumptions:
- Accessible by Super Admin and Building Manager (web only).
- Canvas overlay on PDF. Click on canvas => select asset => place pin at coordinates.
- Tags are saved as (x, y) coordinates.
Fields/Components, Component Type, Requi:
Screen name: Visual Asset Tagging (Edit mode)
- [Edit Tags] - Button - Primary - Enters tag editing mode
- Canvas overlay - Transparent layer over PDF viewer
- Click on empty canvas area => Add Tag popup:
  + Asset - Dropdown - Required - Searchable - Assets in selected building
  + Place Tag - Button - Primary
  + Cancel - Button - Secondary
- Save All Changes - Button - Primary
- Discard Changes - Button - Secondary
User Flow:
Flow: Place Asset Tag on Drawing (Super Admin / BM)

Preconditions:
- Super Admin or Building Manager authenticated (web only).
- A drawing must be loaded for the selected building.
- User must enter Edit mode via [Edit Tags] button.

1. User clicks [Edit Tags] => editing mode activated:
   + [Edit Tags] button changes to [Save All Changes] and [Discard Changes].
   + Canvas overlay becomes interactive.
   + Existing tag pins become movable/removable.

2. User clicks on an empty area of the canvas:
   + Add Tag popup appears:
    - Asset dropdown (required, searchable, shows assets in the selected building only).
    - [Place Tag] button (Primary).
    - [Cancel] button.

3. User searches and selects an asset from the dropdown:
   3.1. Asset already has a tag on this drawing => warning in dropdown: "This asset already has a tag. Placing a new tag will not remove the existing one."
   3.2. User selects a valid asset => [Place Tag] becomes enabled.

4. User clicks [Place Tag]:
   + Pin is placed at the clicked (x, y) coordinates (STAGED - not yet saved to database).
   + A "pending save" indicator may show on the pin (e.g., slightly transparent).
   + Add Tag popup closes.
   + User can immediately place another tag by clicking again on the canvas.

5. User clicks [Save All Changes]:
   5.1. System sends all staged tag placements to the database in a single request.
   5.2. Save succeeds => all pending pins become permanent => "pending save" indicators removed => success toast: "Asset tags saved successfully."
   5.3. Save fails => error: "Failed to save asset tags. Please try again." => staged changes remain (user can retry).

6. User clicks [Discard Changes]:
   + Confirmation prompt: "Discard all unsaved tag changes? This cannot be undone."
   + User confirms => all staged (unsaved) pins are removed => editing mode exits => existing saved tags remain.
   + User cancels => remains in editing mode with staged changes.

7. User clicks [Cancel] in Add Tag popup => popup closes, no pin placed at that click.

Edge cases:
- User tries to place a tag but the asset dropdown is empty (no assets in selected building) => dropdown shows: "No assets found for this building."
- User places 50+ tags in one session without saving => all remain staged until [Save All Changes] or [Discard Changes].
- Network error during save => error shown, staged changes preserved for retry.
- User navigates away from the page with unsaved staged changes => browser confirmation prompt: "You have unsaved changes. Are you sure you want to leave?"

Description/Assumptions: - Move an existing tag pin to a new position on the drawing canvas.
Fields/Components, Component Type, Requi:
Edit mode (active from 1.5.22):
- Click existing tag pin => options shown including [Move tag]
- [Move tag] => pin becomes draggable => drag to new position => release
User Flow:
Flow: Edit Asset Tag Position (Super Admin / BM)

Preconditions:
- Super Admin or Building Manager authenticated (web only).
- Drawing must be loaded with existing tag pins.
- Must be in Edit mode (entered via [Edit Tags]).

1. User is in Edit mode on the drawing.

2. User clicks an existing tag pin:
   + Options menu appears near the pin:
    - [Move tag] button.
    - [Remove tag] button (Danger).

3. User clicks [Move tag]:
   + Pin becomes draggable (cursor changes to grab icon).
   + User clicks and drags pin to new position on canvas.
   + User releases mouse => pin snaps to new position (STAGED - not yet saved).

4. User clicks [Save All Changes]:
   4.1. All staged position changes saved to database.
   4.2. Success toast: "Asset tags saved successfully."
   4.3. If save fails => error: "Failed to save asset tags. Please try again."

5. User clicks [Discard Changes]:
   + All staged moves reverted => pins return to last saved positions.

Edge cases:
- User drags a pin to the very edge of the canvas => system clips coordinates to canvas boundaries (pin cannot be placed outside the drawing area).
- User moves a pin that has never been saved before (just placed in same session) => the final position after move is what gets saved.
- User attempts to move a pin while the canvas is still loading => move interaction disabled until canvas is fully rendered.

Description/Assumptions: - Remove a specific tag pin from the drawing.
Fields/Components, Component Type, Requi:
Edit mode:
- Click tag pin => [Remove tag] button shown

Popup: Confirm Remove Tag
- Message: "Remove tag for [Asset Name]?"
- Remove - Danger / Cancel
User Flow:
Flow: Remove Asset Tag from Drawing (Super Admin / BM)

Preconditions:
- Super Admin or Building Manager authenticated (web only).
- Drawing must be loaded with existing tag pins.
- Must be in Edit mode.

1. User is in Edit mode on the drawing.

2. User clicks an existing tag pin => options menu appears: [Move tag] / [Remove tag].

3. User clicks [Remove tag]:
   + Confirmation popup:
    - Message: "Remove tag for [Asset Name]? The asset itself is not affected."
    - Buttons: [Remove] (Danger) / [Cancel] (Secondary).

4. User clicks [Remove]:
   + Pin is removed from the canvas (STAGED - not yet saved).
   + The asset itself is NOT deactivated or affected in any way.

5. User clicks [Save All Changes]:
   + Removal is persisted to database.
   + Success toast: "Asset tags saved successfully."

6. User clicks [Discard Changes]:
   + Removed pin reappears at its original position.

7. User clicks [Cancel] in confirmation popup => popup closes, pin remains.

Edge cases:
- User removes a pin that was placed in the same session (not yet saved) => the pin simply disappears from staged state; nothing to remove from database.
- Network error during save => "Failed to save asset tags. Please try again." => pin removal is not persisted.

Description/Assumptions:
- Read-only tag view for all roles with drawing access.
- Tap/click a tag pin => asset info popup.
Fields/Components, Component Type, Requi:
Read-only canvas overlay (default mode - not in Edit mode):
- Tag pins - positioned at saved coordinates
- Tag popup (on click/tap):
  + Asset Name / Asset Code / Asset Type / Asset System
  + Current Status - Badge
  + Last Maintenance Date
  + Link to Asset Detail - Hyperlink
  + Close popup - Icon button
User Flow:
Flow: View Asset Tags on Drawing (All roles with drawing access)

Preconditions:
- User authenticated with a role that has drawing view access (Super Admin, Building Manager, MSP Supervisor on web; all roles on mobile read-only).
- Drawing must be uploaded for the selected building.
- NOT in Edit mode (read-only view).

1. User views the drawing (default state when navigating to Drawing screen with a drawing loaded).

2. Tag pins are rendered at their saved coordinates on the drawing canvas.
   + If drawing has 0 tags => drawing is still shown without any pins (no empty state message for tags).

3. User clicks or taps a tag pin:
   + Popup appears showing:
    - Asset Name (bold)
    - Asset Code
    - Asset Type
    - Asset System
    - Current Status badge (Active green / Inactive amber)
    - Last Maintenance Date (or "No maintenance history" if none)
    - [Link to Asset Detail] hyperlink
    - [X] Close button.

4. User clicks [Link to Asset Detail]:
   + System navigates to the Asset Detail page (1.5.13) for that asset.
   + User can navigate back to return to the drawing.

5. User clicks [X] or clicks outside the popup => popup closes.

6. User can continue panning/zooming while popup is closed.

7. Multiple pins in close proximity:
   + If pins overlap, clicking on the overlapping area => popup for the topmost pin is shown.
   + User may need to zoom in to click individual pins in dense areas.

Edge cases:
- Tag for a deactivated asset => popup still shows with Inactive badge. [Link to Asset Detail] still works.
- Tag pin coordinates are outside current visible viewport => pin not visible until user pans to that area.
- Drawing has 100+ tags => all pins rendered; performance may degrade on very large drawings with many tags (optimization TBC).

Description/Assumptions:
- Accessible by Super Admin.
- Lists all spare part categories with search and pagination.
Fields/Components, Component Type, Requi:
Screen name: Spare Part Categories
- Search - Placeholder: "Search by name or code"
- [+ New Category] - Button - Primary
- Table: Category Name / Code / Unit of Measure / Description / Actions (Edit | Delete)
- Empty state: "No spare part categories found."
- Pagination

Screen: Spare Part Category List (Super Admin)

2. System loads the category list:
   + Columns: Code / Name / Unit of Measure / Description / Status / Actions (Edit / Delete).

Description/Assumptions: - Fields: Name (required, unique), Code (required, unique), Unit of Measure (required), Description (optional).
Fields/Components, Component Type, Requi:
Popup: Create Category
- Category Name - Text field - Required - Max 100 chars - Unique
- Category Code - Text field - Required - Max 50 chars - Unique
- Unit of Measure - Text field - Required - Max 50 chars - e.g., pcs, litre
- Description - Textarea - Optional - Max 255 chars
- Cancel / Save
User Flow:
Flow: Create Spare Part Category (Super Admin)

Preconditions:
- Super Admin authenticated.

1. Super Admin clicks [+ New Category] => popup opens.

2. Super Admin fills:
   - Name (required)
   - Code (required)
   - Unit of Measure (required, e.g., "pcs", "litre", "kg")
   - Description (optional)

3. Super Admin clicks [Save]:
   3.1. Name empty => error: "Category name is required." => does NOT create.
   3.2. Name > 100 characters => error: "Name must not exceed 100 characters." => does NOT create.
   3.3. Name duplicate (case-insensitive) => error: "A category with this name already exists." => does NOT create.
   3.4. Code empty => error: "Code is required." => does NOT create.
   3.5. Code duplicate => error: "A category with this code already exists." => does NOT create.
   3.6. Unit of Measure empty => error: "Unit of Measure is required." => does NOT create.
   3.7. All valid:

    + List refreshes to show new category at correct alphabetical position.
    + Success toast: "Category created successfully."
    + Popup closes.

4. Super Admin clicks [Cancel] => popup closes, no category created.

Edge cases:
- Network error => "Failed to create category. Please try again." => no partial record.

Description/Assumptions: - Edit icon on category row => pre-filled popup.
Fields/Components, Component Type, Requi: Popup: Edit Category - same fields as Create, pre-filled.
User Flow:
Flow: Edit Spare Part Category (Super Admin)

Preconditions:
- Super Admin authenticated.

1. Super Admin clicks Edit icon on a category row => pre-filled popup opens.

2. Super Admin modifies Name, Code, Unit of Measure, or Description.

3. Super Admin clicks [Save]:
   3.1. Name empty => error: "Category name is required." => does NOT save.
   3.2. Name duplicate (excluding current category) => error: "A category with this name already exists." => does NOT save.
   3.3. Code empty => error: "Code is required." => does NOT save.
   3.4. Code duplicate (excluding current) => error: "A category with this code already exists." => does NOT save.
   3.5. Unit of Measure empty => error: "Unit of Measure is required." => does NOT save.
   3.6. All valid => record updated => list refreshes => success toast: "Category updated successfully."

4. Super Admin clicks [Cancel] => popup closes, no change.

Edge cases:
- Super Admin changes UoM for a category that already has spare parts => the UoM change applies to the category; existing spare part records are not retroactively updated. Flagged for BA review.
- Network error => "Failed to update category. Please try again."

Description/Assumptions: - Hard-delete. Blocked if spare parts are linked.
Fields/Components, Component Type, Requi:
Popup: Confirm Delete Category
- Message: "Delete [Name]? This cannot be undone."
- Delete (Danger) / Cancel
User Flow:
Flow: Delete Spare Part Category (Super Admin)

Preconditions:
- Super Admin authenticated.

1. Super Admin clicks Delete icon on a category row.
