User Flow:
System Process: Stock-Out (System-triggered)

This is NOT a manual user action. Stock-Out is automatically triggered by the system.

--- Trigger: BM Approves a Spare Part Request ---

1. Building Manager approves a spare part request (via BM Portal or Mobile).

2. System processes the approval:
   2.1. System retrieves the On-Hold reservation for this WO + spare part combination.
   2.2. System verifies current On-Hand quantity:
    + Sufficient (On-Hand - other On-Holds >= requested quantity):
     - On-Hold status for this reservation => Consumed.
     - On-Hand Quantity decreases by the approved quantity.
     - Stock-Out transaction recorded in history log: Date / Spare Part / Quantity / WO ID / Processed By (system).
     - Available quantity (On-Hand - On-Hold) updates.
    + Insufficient (stock was consumed by another WO in between):
     - Does NOT perform stock-out.
     - Marks the request as Unavailable.
     - BM notified: "Spare part [Name] is no longer available for Work Order [ID]. Stock has been consumed by another request."

--- Trigger: WO Cancelled After Stock-Out ---

3. If a WO is cancelled after its spare part request was already approved and stock-out occurred:
   + System performs a stock-out reversal.
   + On-Hand Quantity increases back by the reversed amount.
   + Stock-Out record is marked as "Reversed" in history log.
   + Available quantity updates.

--- Admin Monitoring View ---

4. Super Admin can view Stock-Out history (1.6.13) but cannot manually trigger or reverse stock-out directly.
5. Manual reversal (exceptional case) requires IT or database admin intervention - flagged for BA review.

Edge cases:
- BM approves the same request twice (e.g., system glitch) => system is idempotent: second approval detects already-Consumed status and does nothing.
- Concurrent BM approvals for two different WOs requesting the same part when only enough stock for one => first approval succeeds, second triggers Unavailable flow.

Description/Assumptions:

Fields/Components, Component Type, Requi:
Screen name: On-Hold Inventory (monitoring view)
- Filter: Spare Part / Work Order / Status (All / Active / Released / Consumed)

User Flow:
System Process: Reserve Spare Part On-Hold (System-triggered)

This is NOT a manual user action. On-Hold reservation is automatically managed by the system.

--- Trigger A: Spare Part Request Submitted ---

CONFIRMED: On-Hold is set IMMEDIATELY at submission time (before BM approval). This prevents double-booking of the same stock across multiple WOs.

1. A Technician or Supervisor submits a spare part request for a WO (selecting "Use IMS Stock" option).

2. System processes the submission:
   2.1. System checks current Available stock for the requested spare part:
    + Available = On-Hand - current total On-Hold for all other reservations.
   2.2. Available >= requested quantity:
    + On-Hold reservation created: Spare Part / WO ID / Quantity / Status: Active.
    + Available quantity decreases by reserved amount.
    + On-Hold counter increases.
    + Request status: Pending BM approval.
   2.3. Available < requested quantity:
    + On-Hold is NOT set (cannot reserve what doesn't exist).
    + Request flagged as Unavailable.
    + BM notified: "Spare part [Name] is unavailable for Work Order [ID]. Available: [N], Requested: [M]."
    + Unavailable handling flow triggered (1.6.15).

--- Trigger B: Auto-Release of On-Hold ---

3. On-Hold is automatically released in these scenarios:
   3a. WO cancelled (any status) => system releases all On-Hold reservations for that WO => On-Hold decreases => Available increases.
   3b. BM rejects the spare part request => On-Hold released => Available increases.
   3c. BM approves the request => On-Hold converts to Consumed (stock-out executed) => On-Hand decreases => On-Hold decreases.
   3d. Spare part deactivated while On-Hold exists => On-Hold released automatically (flagged for BA review - current assumption).

--- Manual Release by Super Admin (Exceptional Case) ---

4. Super Admin navigates to Inventories => On-Hold view.
5. Super Admin identifies a stuck On-Hold entry (e.g., associated WO is in an error state).
6. Super Admin clicks [Release] on an Active On-Hold entry.
7. Confirmation popup: "Manually release this On-Hold reservation for [WO ID]? This cannot be undone."
8. Super Admin confirms:
   + On-Hold released.
   + Available quantity increases.
   + Stock-Out does NOT occur (manual release is not an approval).
   + History log entry created: "On-Hold manually released by [Admin Name]."
   + Success toast: "On-Hold released."

Edge cases:
- Multiple requests for the same spare part arrive simultaneously => system processes them sequentially (atomic stock check); first request is reserved, subsequent requests may become Unavailable if stock depleted.
- On-Hold for a cancelled WO not released due to system error => manual release by Super Admin (step 4-8 above).

Description/Assumptions:

- WO NOT automatically stopped - BM decides.
- BM can approve continuation (adds manual note) or wait for restock.
Fields/Components, Component Type, Requi:
Screen name: Unavailable Spare Part Requests (read-only monitoring)
- Filter: Building / Work Order
- Table: WO ID / Spare Part / Requested Qty / Available Qty / Shortage / BM Note / Status / Date Flagged
- Status values: Awaiting BM Decision / Approved to Continue / Waiting for Restock

User Flow:
System Process & Flow: Handle Spare Part Unavailable (Super Admin / BM)

Trigger: System detects insufficient Available stock when a spare part request is submitted.

CONFIRMED: When a spare part is unavailable, the Work Order is NOT blocked. The Technician can continue executing other checklist items. The unavailable part is flagged and BM is notified to decide how to proceed (Approve Continuation or Wait for Restock).

--- System Process ---

1. System marks the spare part request as Unavailable.
2. System generates an in-app notification and email to Building Manager:
   - Subject: "Spare Part Unavailable - Work Order [ID]"
   - Body: "Spare part [Name] (Code: [Code]) is unavailable for Work Order [ID]. Requested: [N] units. Available: [M] units."

--- BM Decision Flow ---

3. BM receives notification and navigates to the WO Detail or Unavailable Requests view.

4. BM reviews the shortage details:
   + WO ID / Spare Part / Requested Quantity / Available Quantity / Shortage Amount.

5. BM selects one of two actions:

   5a. Approve Continuation (proceed without this spare part for now):
    + BM taps/clicks [Approve Continuation].
    + BM can optionally add a note explaining the decision.
    + Request status => Approved to Continue.
    + WO is NOT blocked; Technician can proceed with other checklist items.
    + Supervisor notified: "BM has approved continuation of Work Order [ID] without [Spare Part Name]."
    + The missing spare part may be recorded as Purchase Separately or noted in the Part Replacement table.

   5b. Wait for Restock:
    + BM selects [Wait for Restock] or leaves the request in Unavailable state.
    + Request status => Waiting for Restock.
    + WO remains in its current status (not cancelled or blocked automatically).
    + When stock is replenished (Stock-In occurs that brings Available >= requested quantity):
     - System automatically checks all Unavailable requests.
     - Notifies BM and Supervisor: "Spare part [Name] is now available. Requested quantity can now be fulfilled."
     - BM must re-visit the request to approve the stock-out.

--- Admin Monitoring View ---

6. Super Admin can view all Unavailable requests across all buildings in read-only mode.
7. Super Admin cannot take BM actions (Approve Continuation / Wait for Restock) on behalf of BM.

Edge cases:
- BM is unavailable and WO is urgent => no automatic escalation in MVP. Manual escalation required.
- Stock becomes available but then is consumed by another WO before BM acts => request returns to Unavailable state and BM is notified again.
- Multiple WOs have Unavailable requests for the same part => when stock arrives, system processes them in order of submission date (FIFO - pending confirmation).

Description/Assumptions:
- SYSTEM-TRIGGERED after any stock deduction event.
- If On-Hand falls below Minimum Stock Threshold => alert generated.
- Recipients: Super Admin + Building Manager (in-app + email).
- Alert fires once per threshold breach.
Fields/Components, Component Type, Requi:
This is a system-triggered automation. No dedicated UI screen.

Notification content:
"Low stock alert: [Spare Part Name] is below the minimum threshold. Current stock: [N]. Minimum: [M]."

Delivery: in-app notification + email (AWS SES / SendGrid)
User Flow:
System Process: Low-Stock Alert (System-triggered)

This is NOT a manual user action. Low-Stock Alert is automatically triggered by the system.

--- Trigger: After Any Stock Deduction Event ---

1. A stock deduction event occurs:
   + BM approves a spare part request => stock-out executed.
   + Stock-In error/reversal reduces stock.
   + Manual stock adjustment (if supported - flagged for BA review).

2. System compares updated On-Hand quantity against Minimum Stock Threshold for the affected spare part:
   2.1. On-Hand >= Minimum Stock Threshold => no alert generated.
   2.2. On-Hand < Minimum Stock Threshold:
    + System checks if an alert was already sent for the current breach cycle:
     - If this is the first time On-Hand dropped below threshold since last replenishment => generates alert.
     - If alert was already sent for this breach cycle (stock never went back above threshold) => does NOT send duplicate alert.
    + Alert generated:
     - In-app notification to Super Admin: "Low stock alert: [Spare Part Name] (Code: [Code]). Current stock: [N]. Threshold: [M]."
     - In-app notification to Building Manager(s) for buildings where this part is relevant.
     - Email notification sent via AWS SES/SendGrid to the same recipients.

3. Breach cycle reset:
   + When On-Hand is replenished (Stock-In) and rises above the Minimum Stock Threshold again => the breach cycle is reset.
   + If stock drops below threshold again after replenishment => a new alert is generated.

--- Minimum Stock Threshold Configuration ---

4. CONFIRMED: Minimum Stock Threshold is defined ON THE SPARE PART RECORD ITSELF (one global threshold per part, applies across all buildings). BM receives alerts when On-Hand in their building drops below this threshold.

Edge cases:
- Alert email delivery fails (SES/SendGrid error) => in-app notification still sent; email failure logged. System retries email up to 3 times.
- Spare part has no Minimum Stock Threshold set (threshold = 0 or null) => no alert generated (0 is never less than 0).
- Multiple stock-out events in quick succession drop stock well below threshold => only 1 alert per breach cycle (not one per event).

User Flow:
System Process: Auto-Generate Work Orders from Maintenance Plan

This is NOT a manual user action. WO auto-generation is triggered by the system scheduler.

--- Trigger: Scheduled Cycle Due ---

1. System scheduler runs at a defined interval (e.g., daily at midnight) and checks all Active Maintenance Plans.

2. For each Active plan whose next cycle date has arrived:
   2.1. System iterates through every asset in the plan's Assets tab.
   2.2. For each asset, system creates a new Work Order pre-filled with:
    + WO Type: Maintenance Scheduling.
    + Asset: linked asset (Code, Name, Classification, Location).
    + Building, Floor, Area/Unit from the asset record.
    + Work Checklist: snapshot of the Asset Type's current checklist at generation time (pending client confirmation - flagged for BA review).
    + Round Number: incremented from the previous round (R1, R2, R3...).
    + Maintenance Plan reference: Plan ID + Plan Name + Frequency.
    + Time Required to Complete: from plan settings.
    + Description and Remark: from plan settings.

3. WO initial status determination:
   3.1. Main Technician pre-assigned in Assets tab for this asset:
    + WO Status => Assigned.
    + Main Technician receives push notification: "You have a new Work Order [ID] assigned from Maintenance Plan [Name]."
   3.2. No Main Technician assigned:
    + WO Status => Pending - Unassigned.
    + MSP Supervisor(s) within the relevant User Group notified: "New Work Orders from Maintenance Plan [Name] require technician assignment."

4. Building Manager approval NOT required for Maintenance Plan-generated WOs (plan-level approval covers all WOs from this plan).

5. System updates the plan's Round tab:
   + New round record created: Round No / Start Date / End Date / Total WOs / Status: In Progress.

6. System calculates the next cycle date:
   + Monthly: same day of month, next month.
   + Quarterly: same day, 3 months later.
   + Yearly: same day, 12 months later.
   + If next cycle date falls on a non-business day => pending client confirmation (generate on the day, move to next business day, or move to previous business day). Flagged for BA review.

7. Concurrent plan generation:
   + If multiple plans are due on the same day => system processes them sequentially.
   + If a plan fails to generate WOs (error) => system logs the error and retries (retry count TBC). Does NOT affect other plans.

8. Overlapping rounds:
   + If Round 1 WOs are not yet all Closed when Round 2 is due => system generates Round 2 WOs regardless (pending client confirmation - flagged for BA review).

Edge cases:
- Asset was deactivated after being added to the plan => WO is NOT generated for deactivated assets.
- Pre-assigned Technician was deactivated => WO generated with Status: Pending - Unassigned (Technician assignment cleared). Supervisor notified.
- Plan has 0 assets in Assets tab => no WOs generated for this cycle; round record still created with Total WOs = 0.
- System scheduler downtime => if a cycle date is missed due to system downtime, behavior is pending confirmation (generate on recovery, skip, or require manual trigger). Flagged for BA review.

- Confirmed email triggers: submission / BM accepts / BM declines / WO closed.
- CONFIRMED (per status table): Service Request Accepted = Tenant request accepted by BM, forwarded to MSP Supervisor for review. Supervisor reviews and may update before resubmitting to BM for final approval (Pending status). BM then approves => Pending-Unassigned => Supervisor assigns Technician.

User Flow:
Screen: Submit Service Request (Tenant - Public Portal)

Preconditions:
- No authentication required.
- Public URL accessible by anyone with the link.
- At least 1 active Building must exist in the system for the form to be usable.

1. Tenant navigates to the public Service Portal URL.

2. System checks if any active buildings exist:
   2.1. No active buildings => system renders message: "No buildings are currently available. Please contact your building management office." => form not shown.
   2.2. Active buildings exist => system renders the Service Request form.

3. Tenant fills the form:
   - Building (required, dropdown - shows only Active buildings)
   - Floor (optional, cascades from Building - shows floors of selected building)
   - Area/Unit (optional, cascades from Floor)
   - Asset System (required, dropdown - confirmed by client)
   - Asset Sub-system (optional, cascades from Asset System)
   - Asset Type (optional, cascades from Sub-system)
   - Issue Description (required, textarea, max 1000 chars, placeholder: "Please describe the issue in detail")
   - Photos/Attachments (optional, multi-upload, JPG/PNG/PDF, max 5MB each, max 5 files)
   - Contact Name (required, text field, max 100 chars)
   - Contact Phone (required, text field, max 20 chars)
   - Contact Email (required, must follow email format, max 100 chars)

4. Tenant clicks [Submit]:
   4.1. Building not selected => error: "Please select a building." => does NOT submit.
   4.2. Asset System not selected => error: "Please select an asset system." => does NOT submit.
   4.3. Issue Description empty => error: "Please describe the issue." => does NOT submit.
   4.4. Issue Description > 1000 characters => error: "Description must not exceed 1000 characters." => does NOT submit.
   4.5. Contact Name empty => error: "Please enter your name." => does NOT submit.
   4.6. Contact Phone empty => error: "Please enter your phone number." => does NOT submit.
   4.7. Contact Email empty => error: "Please enter your email address." => does NOT submit.
   4.8. Contact Email format invalid => error: "Please enter a valid email address." => does NOT submit.
   4.9. Photo file type not JPG/PNG/PDF => error: "Only JPG, PNG, and PDF files are accepted." => does NOT submit.
   4.10. Photo > 5MB => error: "Each file must not exceed 5 MB." => does NOT submit.
   4.11. More than 5 files attached => error: "You can attach a maximum of 5 files." => does NOT submit.
   4.12. All valid:
    + Service Request record created in database with Status: Tenant Request.
    + Unique Request ID generated.
    + System sends email to Tenant's Contact Email: "Thank you. Your service request [ID] has been received. We will contact you for updates."
    + System sends in-app notification to Building Manager(s) for the selected building: "New service request received for [Building Name]. Request ID: [ID]."
    + System sends notification email to Building Manager(s).
    + System renders Confirmation screen.

5. Confirmation screen:
   + Message: "Your service request has been submitted successfully."
   + Request ID: "[ID]" (displayed prominently).
   + Note: "Please save your Request ID. You will receive email updates at [Contact Email]."
   + [Submit Another Request] button => resets form to empty.

6. Tenant cannot log in to track status => all subsequent updates sent via email only.

Edge cases:
- Tenant submits and immediately closes browser => request still saved (server-side processing complete).
- Tenant submits duplicate requests for the same issue => system does NOT deduplicate; each submission creates a new request. BM will see multiple requests.
- Building Manager email notification fails (SES/SendGrid error) => request is still created; email failure logged; system retries up to 3 times.
- Tenant confirmation email fails => request is still created; system logs the failure.
- Network timeout during submission => Tenant sees loading indicator. If timeout > 15 seconds => error: "Submission timed out. Please try again." => check if request was created before resubmitting (no duplicate guard in MVP).
- Very large attachments (5 × 5MB = 25MB total) => upload may take 10-30 seconds on slow connections; progress indicator shown per file.
- Portal URL shared publicly => anyone with the link can submit. No captcha or bot protection in MVP (flagged for BA review).

Description/Assumptions:
- Accessible by Building Manager. Account created by Super Admin or Building Manager.
- BM does NOT belong to a User Group. Scope is defined by buildings assigned directly to BM account at creation.
- On successful login => Dashboard (3.1.5) scoped to BM's assigned buildings only.
- If a valid session already exists => system skips Login screen and navigates directly to Dashboard.
- Maximum 5 consecutive failed login attempts before temporary lockout (15 minutes).
- All connections must use HTTPS/SSL with TLS 1.2+.
Fields/Components, Component Type, Requi:
Screen name: Login (Building Manager Portal)

- EZAxis logo - Display - Positioned top of page
- Email - Text field - Required - Placeholder: "Enter your email" - Max 100 characters - Must follow email format - Error indicator on empty/invalid field
- Password - Text field (masked) - Required - Placeholder: "Enter your password" - Min 8 characters - Error indicator on empty field
- Forgot Password? - Hyperlink - Positioned below password field - Navigates to Forgot Password (3.1.3)
- Login - Button - Primary - Full-width
User Flow:
Screen: Login (Building Manager Portal)

Preconditions:
- No authentication required - entry point for unauthenticated Building Managers.
- BM account must exist with role = Building Manager and at least 1 assigned building.
- If valid session exists => system skips Login and navigates directly to Dashboard (3.1.5).

User navigates to the Building Manager Portal URL.

1. System checks for existing valid session:
   1.1. Valid session exists => navigates to Dashboard (3.1.5) scoped to BM's assigned buildings => flow ends.
   1.2. No valid session => renders Login screen.

2. BM enters email and password => clicks [Login].

3. System validates fields:
   3.1. Email empty => inline error: "Email is required." => does NOT query database => flow stops.
   3.2. Password empty => inline error: "Password is required." => does NOT query database => flow stops.
   3.3. Email format invalid => inline error: "Please enter a valid email address." => does NOT query database => flow stops.

4. System checks failed attempt counter for this email:
   4.1. >= 5 consecutive failed attempts within last 15 minutes => does NOT authenticate => error: "Too many failed attempts. Please try again in 15 minutes." => flow stops.

5. System queries database:
   5.1. Email not found => does NOT create session => generic error: "Invalid email or password." => increments failed counter => flow stops.
   5.2. Email found but role != Building Manager => does NOT create session => generic error: "Invalid email or password." => increments failed counter => flow stops.
   5.3. Account Status = Inactive => does NOT create session => error: "Your account is not active. Please contact the administrator."
   5.3. Account Status = Pending (invitation not yet accepted) => does NOT create session => error: "Your account is not yet activated. Please check your email for the invitation link."
   5.4. Account Status = Inactive => error: "Your account is not active. Please contact the administrator." => does NOT increment counter => flow stops.
   5.4. Password does not match => does NOT create session => generic error: "Invalid email or password." => increments failed counter => flow stops.
   5.5. All valid:
    + Session token created and stored server-side.
    + Secure HTTP-only cookie set on client.
    + Failed attempt counter reset to 0.
    + Last login timestamp updated on account.
    + BM navigated to Dashboard (3.1.5) scoped to assigned buildings only.

Edge cases:
- Network error during authentication => error: "Unable to connect. Please check your connection and try again." => no session created => form remains filled.
- BM navigates to Login URL while already authenticated => redirected to Dashboard without showing Login.
- Pasted email with leading/trailing spaces => system trims whitespace before validation.
- BM has no assigned buildings (account misconfigured) => login succeeds but Dashboard shows empty state: "No buildings assigned to your account. Please contact the administrator."

Description/Assumptions:
- Accessible by unauthenticated Building Managers from the Login screen.
- 2-screen flow: Screen 1 - Email input + OTP request; Screen 2 - OTP verification + new password entry.
- OTP sent to the BM's registered email via AWS SES / SendGrid.
- OTP validity: 10 minutes (assumed - flagged for BA review).
- After successful password reset => BM redirected to Login screen (3.1.1).
Fields/Components, Component Type, Requi:
Screen 1 - Enter Email

- Page title - Display - Text: "Forgot Password"
- Instruction text - Display - Text: "Enter your registered email address. We will send you a verification code."
- Email - Text field - Required - Placeholder: "Enter your email" - Max 100 characters - Must follow email format - Error indicator on empty/invalid
- Continue - Button - Primary - Full-width
- Back to Login - Hyperlink - Navigates to Login (3.1.1)

Screen 2 - Enter OTP and New Password

- Page title - Display - Text: "Reset Password"
- Instruction text - Display - Text: "Enter the verification code sent to [email address]."
- OTP Code - Text field - Required - Numeric - 6 digits
- Resend Code - Hyperlink - Active after 60 seconds
- New Password - Text field (masked) - Required - Min 8 characters
- Confirm New Password - Text field (masked) - Required - Must match
- Reset Password - Button - Primary - Full-width
- Back to Login - Hyperlink
User Flow:
Screen: Forgot Password (Building Manager)

Preconditions:
- No authentication required.
- BM must have an active account with the provided email.

Screen 1 - Enter Email:
1. BM clicks [Forgot Password?] on Login => Screen 1.
2. BM enters email => clicks [Continue]:
   2.1. Email empty => error: "Email is required." => flow stops.
   2.2. Email format invalid => error: "Please enter a valid email address." => flow stops.
   2.3. Email not found in BM accounts => does NOT send OTP => error: "No account found with this email address." => flow stops.
   2.4. Account Inactive => does NOT send OTP => error: "Your account is not active. Please contact the administrator." => flow stops.
   2.5. Valid => OTP sent via AWS SES/SendGrid => navigates to Screen 2.
3. BM clicks [Back to Login] => navigates to Login (3.1.1). OTP not sent.

Screen 2 - Enter OTP & New Password:
4. BM enters OTP, New Password, Confirm New Password => clicks [Reset Password]:
   4.1. OTP empty => error: "Please enter the verification code." => flow stops.
   4.2. OTP non-numeric or wrong length => error: "Verification code must be 6 digits." => flow stops.
   4.3. OTP incorrect => error: "Invalid verification code. Please try again." => does NOT reset. After 5 wrong attempts => OTP invalidated => BM must restart from Screen 1.
   4.4. OTP expired (> 10 min) => error: "This code has expired. Please request a new one." => OTP invalidated => returns to Screen 1.
   4.5. New Password empty => error: "New password is required." => flow stops.
   4.6. New Password < 8 chars => error: "Password must be at least 8 characters." => flow stops.
   4.7. Confirm mismatch => error: "Passwords do not match." => flow stops.
   4.8. All valid:
    + Password updated in database.
    + All active sessions for this account immediately invalidated.
    + OTP consumed and cannot be reused.
    + Navigates to Login (3.1.1).
    + Success toast on Login: "Password reset successfully. Please log in with your new password."
5. BM clicks [Resend Code] (active after 60s):
   + New OTP generated and sent => previous OTP invalidated.
   + Countdown timer resets.

Edge cases:
- Email delivery fails => error: "Failed to send verification code. Please try again later." => does NOT navigate to Screen 2.
- BM resets password successfully => all other devices where BM was logged in are automatically signed out.

Description/Assumptions:

WO Status reference (per product document):
Group: Preparation => Draft
Group: New => Technician Request / Tenant Request / Service Request Accepted / Pending
Group: Pending => Unassigned (WO approved, ready for Technician assignment)
Group: In Progress => Assigned / Started
Group: Review => Completed (Supervisor reviews and signs off before forwarding to BM)
Group: Approval => Verified (BM reviews, signs, and approves to close)
Group: Finalized => Closed / Cancelled
Group: Rejection => Ad-hoc Declined / Approval Rejected / Completion Rejected / Verification Rejected
- View toggle: Table view (default) / Kanban view. Both views show the same data with the same filters applied. Kanban groups cards by Status Group (7 columns: New / Pending / In Progress / Review / Approval / Finalized / Rejected). View preference saved per user.
Fields/Components, Component Type, Requi:
Screen: Request List
- Filter: Building / Request Type / Status / Date Range + Search
- Table: ID / Type badge / Building / Description / Submitted By / Status+sub-label / Created Date / View icon
- Empty state: "No requests found."
- Pagination
View toggle:
- [Table] - Button / Icon - Secondary - switches to table/list view
- [Kanban] - Button / Icon - Secondary - active state when kanban view selected
- Toggle persists within the session (user preference saved server-side)

Kanban board (when Kanban view active):
- Layout: horizontal scrollable columns, each column = 1 Status Group
- Column order: New | Pending | In Progress | Review | Approval | Finalized | Rejected
- Column header: Group label + card count badge (colored per group)
- Each card shows:
  + WO ID - monospace code - top-left
  + Specific status badge - top-right (e.g. Assigned, Started, Unassigned)
  + WO title / description (truncated to 2 lines)
  + Asset Code + Location
  + WO Type badge: Maintenance (green) / Ad-hoc (blue) / Service Request (amber)
  + Assignee avatar + name (or "—" if unassigned)
  + Due date (red + overdue label if past due)
  + Overdue / needs-action indicator: red left border on card
- Finalized column: shows latest 2-3 cards + collapsed "N more closed" row
- Rejected column cards: red left border accent to signal action required
- Empty column: shows "No work orders" placeholder text
- Filters (Building / Type / Assignee / Search) apply to both Table and Kanban views simultaneously

Preconditions:
- BM authenticated.
- List scoped to BM's assigned buildings only.

1. BM navigates to Work Orders => Requests (or a dedicated Requests tab).

2. System loads all requests for BM's buildings with default filters:
   + Date Range: last 30 days. Status: All. Type: All.
   + Columns: Date / Request ID / Type badge / Building / Asset / Description (truncated) / Status badge / Actions (View).
   + Sorted by creation date descending.

3. Request types and their statuses that appear here:
   + Tenant Request => status: Tenant Request (awaiting BM accept/decline).
   + Technician Ad-hoc => status: Pending (after Supervisor forwarded to BM).
   + Supervisor Ad-hoc => status: Pending (submitted by Supervisor, awaiting BM approval).
   + Service Request Accepted => Tenant request BM accepted, Supervisor reviewing.

4. BM applies filters:
   4.1. Building => filters to specific building.
   4.2. Type => Tenant Request / Ad-hoc WO.
   4.3. Status => filters to selected status.
   4.4. Date Range.

6. BM clicks View icon or row => View Request Detail (3.3.2).

7. Empty state: "No requests found for the selected filters."

8. Pagination: 20 per page.

Edge cases:
- BM's buildings receive many requests daily => list may have 50+ entries; search and filters are essential.
- List fails to load => error: "Failed to load requests. Please refresh."
Toggle between Table and Kanban view:
1. User clicks [Kanban] toggle button (top-right of toolbar).
2. System re-renders the same data as a 7-column kanban board.
   + Same filters (Building / Type / Status / Date Range) remain applied.
   + Columns: New | Pending | In Progress | Review | Approval | Finalized | Rejected.
   + Status Group mapping:
    - New: Technician Request / Tenant Request / Service Request Accepted / Pending
    - Pending: Unassigned
    - In Progress: Assigned / Started
    - Review: Completed
    - Approval: Verified
    - Finalized: Closed / Cancelled
    - Rejected: Ad-hoc Declined / Approval Rejected / Completion Rejected / Verification Rejected
3. User clicks a card => same Request Detail screen (3.3.2) as from table view.
4. User clicks [Table] toggle => returns to table/list view.
5. View preference saved server-side. Persists across page refreshes.

Edge cases:
- Many cards in one column => column scrolls vertically; board scrolls horizontally.
- All requests in Finalized => Finalized column shows all; other columns show empty state.
- Filter applied while in kanban => board re-renders with filtered cards only.

- Same filters as Admin WO List (1.8.3) scoped to BM's buildings
- Table: ID / Type / Asset / Location / Due Date (overdue indicator) / Status / Actions
View toggle:
- [Table] - Button / Icon - Secondary - switches to table/list view
- [Kanban] - Button / Icon - Secondary - active state when kanban view selected
- Toggle persists within the session (user preference saved server-side)

Kanban board (when Kanban view active):
- Layout: horizontal scrollable columns, each column = 1 Status Group
- Column order: New | Pending | In Progress | Review | Approval | Finalized | Rejected
- Column header: Group label + card count badge (colored per group)
- Each card shows:
  + WO ID - monospace code - top-left
  + Specific status badge - top-right (e.g. Assigned, Started, Unassigned)
  + WO title / description (truncated to 2 lines)
  + Asset Code + Location
  + WO Type badge: Maintenance (green) / Ad-hoc (blue) / Service Request (amber)
  + Assignee avatar + name (or "—" if unassigned)
  + Due date (red + overdue label if past due)
  + Overdue / needs-action indicator: red left border on card
- Finalized column: shows latest 2-3 cards + collapsed "N more closed" row
- Rejected column cards: red left border accent to signal action required
- Empty column: shows "No work orders" placeholder text
- Filters (Building / Type / Assignee / Search) apply to both Table and Kanban views simultaneously
User Flow:
Screen: Work Order List (Building Manager)

Preconditions:
- BM authenticated.
- List scoped to BM's assigned buildings only.

1. BM navigates to Work Orders (main WO view).

2. System loads all WOs for BM's buildings with default filters:
   + Status: All. Type: All. Date Range: last 30 days.
   + Columns: WO ID / Type badge / Asset Code / Building / Location / Due Date (overdue in red) / Assigned Technician / Status badge / Actions (View).
   + Sorted by creation date descending.

3. BM applies filters:
   3.1. Building (restricted to assigned buildings).
   3.2. Status: full status list.
   3.3. WO Type: Maintenance Scheduling / Ad-hoc Work / Service Request.
   3.4. Date Range.
   3.5. Filters combinable; [Clear All] resets.

4. BM searches by WO ID or Asset Code => real-time filter.

5. BM clicks WO row or View icon => View WO Detail (3.4.2).

6. Overdue WOs: Due Date in red + overdue indicator on row.

7. Empty state: "No work orders found for the selected filters."

8. Pagination: 20 per page.

Edge cases:
- BM arrives from Dashboard KPI card => list pre-filtered to relevant status group.
- List fails to load => error: "Failed to load work orders. Please refresh."
Toggle between Table and Kanban view:
1. User clicks [Kanban] toggle button.
2. System re-renders WO data as 7-column kanban board.
   + All active filters remain applied.
   + Column mapping identical to 3.3.1 kanban.
3. Overdue WOs: red left border on card + red due date text.
4. Rejected column cards: red left border accent to signal that action is required (Technician must correct for Completion Rejected; Supervisor must resubmit for Verification Rejected).
5. User clicks a card => WO Detail screen (3.4.2).
6. User clicks [Table] => returns to table view.
7. View preference saved server-side.

Edge cases:
- Pending column (Unassigned): Supervisor should assign these immediately — column highlighted with amber column header to indicate pending action.
- Board loads but one status group fails => that column shows "Failed to load. Retry." inline; other columns render normally.
- BM arrives from Dashboard KPI card click => list pre-filtered to relevant status group; kanban opens with that column highlighted.

Feature/Screen: Approve Completed Work Order (Sign Off & Close)
Description/Assumptions:
- Accessible by Building Manager. Scoped to assigned buildings only.
- BM reviews Verified WOs (MSP Supervisor has signed off).
- BM must sign off before WO can be closed.
- Confirmed from WO Execution diagram.
Fields/Components, Component Type, Requi:
Button: [Sign Off & Close] - Primary on WO Detail (status = Verified)
Popup: Confirm Sign Off & Close
- Message: "Sign off and close this work order? This cannot be undone."
- Sign Off & Close - Primary + Cancel
User Flow:
Flow: Approve Completed Work Order - Sign Off & Close (Building Manager)

Preconditions:
- BM authenticated.
- WO must be in Verified status (MSP Supervisor has already reviewed and signed off).
- WO within BM's assigned buildings scope.

1. BM opens WO Detail (3.4.2) for a Verified WO.
2. BM reviews all content: checklist completion, photos evidence, part replacement, sign-off records.
3. BM clicks [Sign Off & Close].
4. Confirmation popup: "Sign off and close Work Order [WO ID]? This action cannot be undone."
5. BM clicks [Confirm]:
   5.1. System checks WO is still in Verified status:
    + Status changed by concurrent action => error: "Work order status has changed. Please refresh." => flow stops.
   5.2. All valid:
    + BM sign-off recorded with timestamp.
    + WO status => Closed (TERMINAL STATE).
    + System does NOT allow any further edits or status changes.
    + MSP Supervisor notified (in-app + email): "Work Order [ID] has been closed by Building Manager."
    + MSP Technician notified: "Work Order [ID] has been closed."
    + If WO was a Service Request => Tenant email sent: "Your service request [ID] has been completed and closed."
    + History log: "Closed by [BM Name] at [timestamp]."
    + Success toast: "Work order closed successfully."
    + Action buttons removed from Detail page; replaced with read-only Closed badge.

6. BM clicks [Cancel] => popup closes, no change. WO remains in Verified status.

Edge cases:
- BM signs off on a WO where the Technician's sign-off is missing (if sign-off was bypassed somehow) => system should validate sign-off chain completeness before allowing BM close (pending confirmation - flagged for BA review).
- Network error => "Failed to close work order. Please try again." => WO remains in Verified status.

Description/Assumptions:
- Accessible by Building Manager. Scoped to assigned buildings only.
- Reject sends WO back to MSP Supervisor only (not Technician).
- Rejection Reason is REQUIRED.
Fields/Components, Component Type, Requi:
Button: [Reject] - Secondary on WO Detail (status = Verified)
Popup: Confirm Reject
- Rejection Reason - Textarea - REQUIRED
- Reject (Danger) + Cancel
User Flow:
Flow: Reject Verified Work Order (Building Manager)

Preconditions:
- BM authenticated.
- WO must be in Verified status.
- WO within BM's assigned buildings scope.

1. BM opens WO Detail (3.4.2) for a Verified WO.
2. BM reviews content and identifies an issue with the work or documentation.
3. BM clicks [Reject].
4. Rejection popup appears:
   + Field: Rejection Reason - Textarea - REQUIRED - max 500 characters.
   + Red border shown on empty submit attempt.
   + Buttons: [Reject] (Danger) / [Cancel] (Secondary).
5. BM enters detailed rejection reason => clicks [Reject]:
   5.1. Reason empty => inline error: "Rejection reason is required." => does NOT reject.
   5.2. Valid:
    + WO status => Verification Rejected.
    + MSP Supervisor notified (in-app + email): "Work Order [ID] has been rejected by Building Manager. Please review and resubmit. Reason: [reason]."
    + MSP Technician is NOT directly notified (Supervisor handles communication with Technician).
    + History log: "Rejected by [BM Name]. Reason: [reason] at [timestamp]."
    + Success toast: "Work order returned to Supervisor for review."
    + Action buttons removed; WO status badge updates to Verification Rejected.
6. BM clicks [Cancel] => popup closes, no change.

Edge cases:
- BM rejects the same WO multiple times (after Supervisor resubmits) => each rejection records a new history entry. Multiple Verification Rejected events are valid.
- Network error => "Failed to reject work order. Please try again." => WO remains in Verified status.

Flow: Create Asset (Building Manager)

Preconditions:
- BM authenticated.
- Buildings, Asset Types, Floors must already exist.

1. BM clicks [+ New Asset] => New Asset page.
2. Fills Classification section: Asset System / Sub-system / Asset Type (cascading dropdowns).

4. Fills Details section: Name / Code / Model / Serial / Brand / Purchase Date / Status / Photos.
5. Clicks [Save]:
   5.1. Asset System not selected => error: "Asset System is required."
   5.2. Asset Type not selected => error: "Asset Type is required."
   5.3. Building not selected => error: "Building is required."
   5.4. Building outside BM's scope => not in dropdown; cannot be selected.
   5.5. Floor not selected => error: "Floor is required."
   5.6. Asset Name empty => error: "Asset name is required."
   5.7. Asset Code empty => error: "Asset code is required."

   5.9. Photo invalid type/size => same validation as Admin (1.5.14).
   5.10. All valid:
    + Asset created. QR code auto-generated.
    + Navigates back to Asset List.
    + Success toast: "Asset created successfully."
6. Clicks [Cancel] => back to list without saving.

Edge cases:
- Selected Building has no Floors => Floor dropdown shows: "No floors available. Please add floors to this building first."

- Accessible by Building Manager. Scoped to BM's assigned buildings only.
- Deactivation (not hard-delete). Asset record and all historical data preserved.
- Deactivated assets no longer available for new WOs or Maintenance Plans.
Fields/Components, Component Type, Requi:

- Message - Display - Text: "Deactivate [Asset Name]? The asset record and history will be preserved."
- Confirm - Button - Danger
- Cancel - Button - Secondary
User Flow:
Flow: Deactivate Asset (Building Manager)

Preconditions:
- BM authenticated. Asset within BM's assigned buildings scope. Asset must be Active.

1. BM clicks Deactivate icon on row or [Deactivate] on Asset Detail.
2. Confirmation popup: "Deactivate [Asset Name]? The record and history will be preserved."
3. BM clicks [Confirm]:
   3.1. System checks for active WOs linked to this asset:
    + Active WOs exist => does NOT deactivate => error: "Cannot deactivate - active work orders are linked to this asset."
   3.2. System checks for active Maintenance Plans including this asset:
    + Active plans include this asset => does NOT deactivate => error: "Cannot deactivate - this asset is included in active maintenance plan(s)."
   3.3. All checks pass:
    + Status => Inactive.
    + Asset removed from all dropdowns for new WO creation.
    + Asset excluded from future Maintenance Plan WO generation.
    + Historical records preserved with Inactive badge.
    + Success toast: "Asset deactivated successfully."

4. BM clicks [Cancel] => no change.

Reactivate:
5. BM opens Inactive asset => [Activate] => confirmation => Status: Active => success: "Asset activated successfully."

Edge cases:
- Asset has only Closed/Cancelled WOs => deactivation allowed.
- Network error => "Failed to deactivate asset. Please try again."

Description/Assumptions:
- Accessible by Building Manager. Scoped to BM's assigned buildings only.
- Allows creating multiple assets via CSV file upload.
- Building filter restricted to BM's assigned buildings.
- Template downloadable. Valid rows inserted; invalid rows returned with error report.
- Each imported asset auto-gets a QR code.
Fields/Components, Component Type, Requi:
Screen: Bulk Upload Asset (Building Manager)

- Page title: "Bulk Upload Asset"
- Instruction text: "Download the template, fill in your asset data, then upload the completed file."
- Download Template - Button - Secondary
- Building - Dropdown - Required - Values: BM's assigned buildings only
- File input - Accepts CSV only - Max 10 MB
- Upload - Button - Primary - Disabled until file selected and building chosen

Import Result (after upload):
- Summary: "[N] assets imported. [M] rows rejected."
- Download Error Report - Button - Conditional (visible if M > 0)
- View Imported Assets - Hyperlink
User Flow:
Flow: Bulk Upload Asset (Building Manager)

Preconditions:
- BM authenticated. Building must be selected (restricted to BM's assigned buildings).

1. BM navigates to Assets => Bulk Upload.
2. Clicks [Download Template] => CSV template downloads.
3. Fills template, saves as CSV.
4. Selects Building from dropdown (BM's buildings only).
5. Selects CSV file => clicks [Upload]:
   5.1. File not CSV => error: "Only CSV files are accepted."
   5.2. File > 10MB => error: "File size must not exceed 10 MB."
   5.3. CSV cannot be parsed => error: "Invalid file format. Please use the provided template."
   5.4. Valid file => system processes row by row.

6. Per-row validation:
   + Required fields missing => row rejected.
   + Asset Code duplicate (in system or within CSV) => row rejected.
   + Asset System/Type/Building/Floor not found => row rejected.
   + Building in CSV row does not match selected building => row rejected.
   + Invalid date format => row rejected.

7. After processing:
   + Valid rows => assets created with auto-generated QR codes.
   + Invalid rows => downloadable error report.
   + Summary shown: "[N] assets imported. [M] rows rejected."
   + If M > 0 => [Download Error Report] button.
   + [View Imported Assets] => Asset List filtered to new assets.

Edge cases:
- All rows invalid => warning: "No assets were imported. Please review the error report."
- CSV has 0 data rows => warning: "The uploaded file contains no data."
- Network error during upload => "Upload failed. Please check your connection and try again." => no partial import.

Edge cases:
- Password-protected PDF => uploads but viewer shows: "This PDF is password protected and cannot be displayed."
- Network disconnects mid-upload => upload fails; previous drawing preserved; error message shown.

User Flow:
System Process: Stock-Out (Building Manager context)

Trigger: BM approves a spare-part request from a Work Order.

1. BM approves spare-part request (in WO Detail or dedicated request view).
2. System processes:
   2.1. Retrieves On-Hold reservation for this WO + spare part.
   2.2. Verifies current On-Hand:
    + Sufficient (On-Hand - other On-Holds >= requested):
     - On-Hold => Consumed.
     - On-Hand decreases by approved quantity.
     - Stock-Out transaction recorded: Date / Part / Quantity / WO ID / Processed By (system).
     - Available updates.
    + Insufficient (stock consumed by another WO in between):
     - Does NOT stock-out.
     - Marks request Unavailable.
     - BM notified: "Spare part [Name] is no longer available for Work Order [ID]. Stock has been consumed by another request."

Stock-Out Reversal:
3. WO cancelled after stock-out already occurred:
   + On-Hand restored by reversed amount.
   + Stock-Out record marked "Reversed" in history.

BM monitoring:
4. BM views Stock-Out history (3.6.9) but cannot manually trigger stock-out.

Edge cases:
- BM approves same request twice (system glitch) => second approval is idempotent (already-Consumed status detected; no action taken).
- Concurrent approvals deplete last stock unit => first wins; second triggers Unavailable.

User Flow:
System Process: Reserve Spare Part On-Hold (Building Manager context)

Trigger: Technician or Supervisor submits a spare-part request for a WO.

1. System checks Available stock (On-Hand - existing On-Holds):
   + Sufficient:
    - On-Hold reservation created: Part / WO ID / Quantity / Status: Active.
    - Available decreases. On-Hold increases.
    - Request status: Pending BM approval.
   + Insufficient:
    - On-Hold NOT set.
    - Request flagged Unavailable.
    - BM notified (in-app + email): "Spare part [Name] is unavailable for Work Order [ID]. Available: [N], Requested: [M]."
    - Unavailable handling flow triggered (3.6.12).

Auto-release triggers:
- WO cancelled => all On-Holds for that WO released.
- BM rejects request => On-Hold released.
- BM approves request => On-Hold => Consumed (stock-out).

BM monitoring view: BM can see On-Hold reservations for their buildings in the Inventories => On-Hold screen.

Edge cases:
- Concurrent requests for same part when only enough for one => first processed wins; second flagged Unavailable.

Description/Assumptions:

Automatic release (system-triggered):
- WO cancelled / BM rejects request / BM approves and stock-out executed.

Manual release by BM (exceptional cases):
1. BM navigates to Inventories => On-Hold view.
2. Identifies stuck On-Hold entry (e.g., WO in error state).
3. BM clicks [Release] on Active On-Hold.

5. BM confirms:

   + Stock-Out does NOT occur.
   + History log: "On-Hold manually released by [BM Name] at [timestamp]."

User Flow:
Flow: Handle Spare Part Unavailable (Building Manager)

Trigger: System marks a spare-part request Unavailable due to insufficient stock.

1. BM receives in-app notification + email: "Spare part [Name] is unavailable for Work Order [ID]. Available: [N], Requested: [M]."
2. BM navigates to WO Detail or Inventories => Unavailable Requests.
3. BM reviews shortage: WO ID / Spare Part / Requested Qty / Available Qty / Shortage.

4. BM chooses action:

4a. Approve Continuation:
   + BM clicks [Approve Continuation].
   + Optional note field.
   + Request status => Approved to Continue.
   + WO NOT blocked; Technician can proceed with other checklist items.
   + Supervisor notified: "BM approved continuation for WO [ID] without [Spare Part Name]."
   + Technician records part as Purchase Separately or notes absence.

4b. Wait for Restock:
   + BM leaves request in Unavailable or selects [Wait for Restock].
   + Status => Waiting for Restock.
   + When Stock-In brings Available >= requested:
    - System checks all pending Unavailable requests.
    - Notifies BM and Supervisor: "Spare part [Name] is now available for WO [ID]."
    - BM revisits to approve stock-out.

Edge cases:
- BM unavailable and WO urgent => no auto-escalation in MVP. Manual follow-up required.
- Stock arrives but another WO claims it first => request returns to Unavailable; BM re-notified.
- FIFO processing when multiple WOs await same part after restock => pending client confirmation.

Description/Assumptions:
- Accessible by Building Manager on mobile (Android & iOS).
- Account created by Super Admin or Building Manager via web portal.
- BM does NOT belong to a User Group. Scope defined by buildings assigned to BM account.
- On successful login => Dashboard (4.1.5) scoped to BM's assigned buildings.
- If a valid session already exists => system skips Login and navigates directly to Dashboard.
- Maximum 5 consecutive failed attempts before 15-minute lockout.
- All connections use HTTPS/SSL with TLS 1.2+.
Fields/Components, Component Type, Requi:
Screen: Login (Building Manager Mobile)

- EZAxis logo - Display - Centered top
- Email - Text field - Required - Keyboard type: email - Placeholder: "Enter your email" - Max 100 characters
- Password - Text field (masked) - Required - Placeholder: "Enter your password" - Min 8 characters - Show/hide password toggle icon
- Forgot Password? - Hyperlink - Below password field - Navigates to Forgot Password (4.1.3)
- Login - Button - Primary - Full-width - Large tap target
- Loading indicator while authenticating
User Flow:
Screen: Login (Building Manager Mobile)

Preconditions:
- No authentication required - entry point for unauthenticated BM on mobile app.
- BM account must exist with role = Building Manager.
- If valid session token is stored on device => system skips Login and navigates directly to Dashboard.

1. App opens. System checks for stored session token on device:
   1.1. Valid session token found => navigates directly to Dashboard (4.1.5) => flow ends.
   1.2. No token or expired token => renders Login screen.

2. BM enters email and password => taps [Login].
   + Keyboard dismisses automatically when [Login] tapped.
   + Loading spinner shown on [Login] button while authenticating.

3. System validates fields:
   3.1. Email empty => inline error below field: "Email is required." => does NOT authenticate.
   3.2. Password empty => inline error: "Password is required." => does NOT authenticate.
   3.3. Email format invalid => inline error: "Please enter a valid email address." => does NOT authenticate.

4. System checks failed attempt counter:
   4.1. >= 5 consecutive failed attempts within last 15 min => does NOT authenticate => error toast: "Too many failed attempts. Please try again in 15 minutes."

5. System queries backend:
   5.1. Email not found or role != Building Manager => does NOT create session => error toast: "Invalid email or password." => increments counter.
   5.2. Account Status = Inactive => does NOT create session => error: "Your account is not active. Please contact the administrator."
   5.3. Account Status = Pending (invitation not yet accepted) => does NOT create session => error: "Your account is not yet activated. Please check your email for the invitation link."
   5.4. Account Status = Inactive => error toast: "Your account is not active. Please contact the administrator."
   5.3. Password mismatch => does NOT create session => error toast: "Invalid email or password." => increments counter.
   5.4. All valid:
    + Session token stored securely on device (Keychain on iOS / Keystore on Android).
    + Failed attempt counter reset to 0.
    + Last login timestamp updated.
    + Navigates to Dashboard (4.1.5) scoped to BM's assigned buildings.

Edge cases:
- Network unavailable when tapping [Login] => error toast: "No internet connection. Please check your network and try again."
- BM's device is offline but has a valid cached session => app opens to last cached state (offline mode) if offline caching is supported; otherwise redirects to Login with message: "You are offline. Please connect to log in."
- BM pastes email with leading/trailing spaces => system trims whitespace before validation.
- BM has no assigned buildings (account misconfiguration) => login succeeds but Dashboard shows: "No buildings assigned to your account. Please contact the administrator."

- Accessible by authenticated Building Manager from any screen in the mobile app.

- Confirmation prompt shown before executing logout.
- On logout: session token immediately invalidated server-side and removed from device.

User Flow:
Flow: Logout (Building Manager Mobile)

Preconditions:
- BM authenticated on mobile app.

1. BM taps Profile or More icon in bottom navigation.
2. Profile/Settings screen opens. BM taps [Sign out] menu item.
3. System shows native OS alert dialog:
   + Title: "Sign Out"
   + Message: "Are you sure you want to sign out?"
   + Buttons: [Sign out] (destructive style) | [Cancel].
4a. BM taps [Sign out]:
   + Session token invalidated server-side immediately.
   + Session token removed from device secure storage (Keychain/Keystore).
   + Any cached data cleared from app local storage.
   + App navigates to Login screen (4.1.1).
4b. BM taps [Cancel]:
   + Alert dismisses.
   + BM remains on Profile/Settings screen, fully authenticated.

Edge cases:
- Network unavailable when logout tapped => system still removes local token and navigates to Login. Server-side invalidation queued and retried when online (or token expires naturally server-side).
- BM's session expires naturally while app is in background => on next foreground action requiring API, system detects 401 response => clears local token => navigates to Login with toast: "Your session has expired. Please log in again."
- App force-closed and reopened => system re-checks stored token on app open (step 1 of Login flow).

User Flow:
Screen: Forgot Password (Building Manager Mobile)

Preconditions:
- No authentication required.
- BM must have an active account with the provided email.

Screen 1 - Enter Email:
1. BM taps [Forgot Password?] on Login screen => Screen 1 loads.
2. BM enters email (keyboard type: email) => taps [Continue]:
   2.1. Empty => error: "Email is required."
   2.2. Format invalid => error: "Please enter a valid email address."
   2.3. Email not found in BM accounts => does NOT send OTP => error: "No account found with this email address."
   2.4. Account Inactive => does NOT send OTP => error: "Your account is not active. Please contact the administrator."
   2.5. Network unavailable => error: "No internet connection. Please check your network and try again."
   2.6. Valid => OTP sent via AWS SES/SendGrid => navigates to Screen 2.
3. BM taps [Back to Login] => navigates to Login (4.1.1). No OTP sent.

Screen 2 - Enter OTP & New Password:
4. Screen shows masked email OTP was sent to.
5. BM enters OTP (numeric keyboard, 6 digits) / New Password / Confirm:
   5.1. OTP empty => error: "Please enter the verification code."
   5.2. OTP wrong format => error: "Verification code must be 6 digits."
   5.3. OTP incorrect => error: "Invalid verification code. Please try again." After 5 wrong attempts => OTP invalidated => must restart from Screen 1.
   5.4. OTP expired (> 10 min) => error: "This code has expired. Please request a new one." => returns to Screen 1.
   5.5. Password < 8 chars => error: "Password must be at least 8 characters."
   5.6. Confirm mismatch => error: "Passwords do not match."
   5.7. Network unavailable => error: "No internet connection."
   5.8. All valid:
    + Password updated.
    + All active sessions (web + other mobile devices) invalidated.
    + Navigates to Login (4.1.1).
    + Success toast: "Password reset successfully. Please log in with your new password."
6. [Resend Code] (active after 60s with countdown timer):
   + New OTP sent => previous OTP invalidated => countdown resets.

Edge cases:
- Email delivery fails => "Failed to send verification code. Please try again later."
- BM closes app mid-reset and reopens => returns to Login screen (Screen 2 state not persisted).

Description/Assumptions:
- Accessible by authenticated Building Manager from any screen.
- Supports English (EN) and Vietnamese (VI).
- Language switch applies immediately to all UI elements without app restart.
- Selected language persisted per user account across sessions and devices.
Fields/Components, Component Type, Requi:
Component: Language Switcher (Building Manager Mobile)

Access path: Profile / Settings => Language

- Language - Selection list or toggle
- Options: English (EN) | Ti?ng Vi?t (VI)
- Currently selected language highlighted
- Tap to change - applies immediately
User Flow:
Flow: Multi-Language Switch (Building Manager Mobile)

Preconditions:
- BM authenticated.
- Language setting accessible from Profile/Settings.

1. BM navigates to Profile => Settings => Language.
2. Current language highlighted in a selection list.
3. BM taps alternate language (EN or VI):
   3.1. App immediately re-renders all UI text in selected language without restart.
   3.2. Data content (asset names, descriptions) is NOT translated.
   3.3. System saves preference to BM's account via API.
   3.4. Save succeeds => preference persists across sessions and devices (web portal also reflects change).
   3.5. Save fails (network issue) => UI still shows new language for current session => error toast: "Failed to save language preference. The change will apply to this session only." => reverts to previous on next login.

Edge cases:
- BM switches language while a form is open (e.g., during Create WO) => field values preserved; labels/placeholders update.
- BM switches language while offline => UI changes locally but server sync fails => preference reverts on next login.

Description/Assumptions:
- Simplified dashboard - KPI cards only, no complex charts.
- Scoped to BM's assigned buildings.
- CONFIRMED MOBILE SCOPE: Status-transition actions available. Create/Edit/Deactivate = web-only.
Fields/Components, Component Type, Requi:
Screen: Dashboard (BM Mobile)
- Building selector - BM's buildings
- KPI cards: Total Requests / Pending Approval / In Progress WOs / Completed WOs
- Recent Activity list - Latest 5 updates
- Bottom navigation: Home / Requests / Work Orders / Assets / More
User Flow:
Screen: Dashboard (Building Manager Mobile)

Preconditions:
- BM authenticated. Dashboard is default landing screen.
- Scoped to BM's assigned buildings.

1. App opens to Dashboard. System loads with defaults:
   + Building: first assigned building.
   + Date Range: last 30 days.

2. System fetches and renders:
   2.1. KPI cards (tappable, large tap targets):
    + Total Requests / Pending Approval / In Progress WOs / Completed WOs / Closed WOs.
    + Each card shows count + label. Count highlighted in bold.
   2.2. Recent Activity list:
    + Latest 5-10 activity events (new requests, WO status changes, alerts).
    + Each item: event type icon / description / timestamp / status badge.
    + "See all" link => navigates to full filtered list.
   2.3. Note: No complex charts on mobile (Gantt and Trend charts are web-only). Mobile shows KPI + recent activity only.

3. BM taps Building selector (dropdown or picker):
   + Only BM's assigned buildings shown.
   + Selects building => KPI cards re-fetch scoped to that building.

4. BM taps a KPI card:
   4.1. "Pending Approval" => navigates to Request List filtered to Pending status (no Request List screen on mobile but navigates to 4.3 view).
   4.2. "In Progress WOs" => navigates to WO List filtered to Assigned + Started (4.4.1).
   4.3. "Closed WOs" => navigates to WO List filtered to Closed (4.4.1).

5. BM taps a Recent Activity item => navigates to the relevant Request or WO Detail screen.

6. BM pulls down to refresh => re-fetches all data.

7. Empty states:
   + No recent activity => "No recent activity."
   + No buildings assigned => "No buildings assigned. Please contact the administrator."

Edge cases:
- BM has only 1 building => Building selector not shown (pre-selected and fixed).
- API timeout on section load => section shows: "Failed to load. Tap to retry."
- Dashboard loads in offline mode => shows cached data with banner: "Showing cached data. Pull to refresh when online."

   + Avatar: tap current avatar => options appear: "Take Photo" (camera) / "Choose from Library" (gallery).

   7.4. Confirm mismatch => error: "Passwords do not match."

- BM takes photo for avatar and camera permission not granted => app shows system permission request. If denied => error: "Camera permission is required to take a photo. Please enable it in your device settings."

Preconditions:
- BM authenticated. Request in Pending status. Within BM's assigned buildings scope.

Description/Assumptions:
- FCM delivery.
- Trigger events: New Tenant Request / New Ad-hoc WO (Pending) / WO ready for sign-off (Verified) / Spare part request needs approval / Low-stock alert / Maintenance Plan submitted (Pending).
Fields/Components, Component Type, Requi:
Push notification (device OS) + in-app badge
Notification list (bell icon):
- Each entry: icon / title / description / timestamp / read indicator
- Tap => navigates to relevant screen
User Flow:
System Process: Push Notification (Building Manager Mobile)

This is a system-triggered process, not a user-initiated flow.

--- Delivery mechanism ---

1. System event occurs that triggers a notification for BM.
2. Backend sends push notification payload to Firebase Cloud Messaging (FCM).
3. FCM routes the notification to all registered BM devices for this account.
4. Device is online => notification delivered immediately to device.
5. Device is offline => FCM queues the notification for delivery when device comes online. FCM message delivery is best-effort; messages may expire after 28 days if device remains offline.

--- Trigger events and notification content ---

Events that trigger BM mobile notifications:
+ New Tenant Request received for BM's building:
  Title: "New Service Request"
  Body: "A tenant has submitted a service request for [Building Name]. Tap to review."
  Action: opens Request Detail on tap.

+ New Ad-hoc WO Request pending BM approval (Supervisor submitted):
  Title: "Work Order Awaiting Approval"
  Body: "An ad-hoc work order request for [Building Name] requires your approval."
  Action: opens Request Detail.

+ WO ready for BM sign-off (status = Verified):
  Title: "Work Order Ready for Sign-off"
  Body: "Work Order [ID] has been reviewed by the Supervisor and is ready for your final sign-off."
  Action: opens WO Detail.

+ Spare part request awaiting BM approval (On-Hold or Unavailable):
  Title: "Spare Part Request"
  Body: "A spare part request for Work Order [ID] requires your approval."
  Action: opens WO Detail (spare parts tab).

+ Low-stock alert for spare part in BM's building:
  Title: "Low Stock Alert"
  Body: "Spare part [Name] stock is below minimum threshold. Current stock: [N]."
  Action: opens Spare Part Detail.

+ Maintenance Plan submitted by Supervisor (Pending approval):
  Title: "Maintenance Plan Pending Approval"
  Body: "A maintenance plan requires your approval for [Building Name]."
  Action: opens Plan Detail.

--- In-app notification handling ---

6. When app is open (foreground): notification displayed as in-app banner at top of screen. Auto-dismisses after 4 seconds. Tap to navigate.

7. When app is in background: OS displays notification in system tray. Tap to open app and navigate to relevant screen. Badge count increments on app icon.

8. When app is closed: OS displays notification. Tap to open app (cold start) and navigate to relevant detail screen.

9. BM taps notification from any state:
   + App opens (or comes to foreground).
   + Navigates directly to the relevant detail screen (Request Detail, WO Detail, Spare Part Detail, or Plan Detail).
   + Notification marked as read.
   + Badge count decrements.

10. BM has unread notifications: notification bell icon in app header shows badge count.

11. Notification permissions:
    + On first app install: system requests notification permission.
    + If BM denies => push notifications not delivered. In-app prompts user to enable in device settings.
    + BM can manage notification settings in device Settings => App => Notifications.

Edge cases:
- FCM token expires or device registration changes (e.g., app reinstall) => system refreshes FCM token on next app open and re-registers with backend.
- BM receives notification for a request that has already been processed by another session => tapping notification navigates to Detail screen which shows the current (already-processed) status.
- Multiple notifications received while offline => all queued and delivered in batch when device comes online. App processes them sequentially.
- BM logs out => FCM token unregistered => no more notifications until next login.

Description/Assumptions:
- Accessible by MSP Supervisor. Account created by Super Admin or Building Manager.
- Supervisor MUST belong to a User Group. Scope defined by User Group: buildings + asset systems.
- On successful login => Dashboard (5.1.6) scoped to Supervisor's User Group.
- If valid session exists => system skips Login and navigates directly to Dashboard.
- Maximum 5 consecutive failed attempts before 15-minute lockout.
- All connections use HTTPS/SSL with TLS 1.2+.
Fields/Components, Component Type, Requi:
Screen name: Login (MSP Supervisor Portal)

- EZAxis logo - Display - Positioned top of page
- Email - Text field - Required - Placeholder: "Enter your email" - Max 100 characters - Must follow email format - Error indicator on empty/invalid
- Password - Text field (masked) - Required - Placeholder: "Enter your password" - Min 8 characters - Error indicator on empty
- Forgot Password? - Hyperlink - Below password field - Navigates to Forgot Password (5.1.3)
- Login - Button - Primary - Full-width
User Flow:
Screen: Login (MSP Supervisor Portal)

Preconditions:
- No authentication required - entry point for unauthenticated MSP Supervisors.
- Account must exist with role = MSP Supervisor AND must belong to a User Group.
- If valid session exists => system skips Login and navigates directly to Dashboard (5.1.6).

User navigates to the MSP Supervisor Portal URL.

1. System checks for existing valid session:
   1.1. Valid session => navigates to Dashboard (5.1.6) scoped to Supervisor's User Group => flow ends.
   1.2. No valid session => renders Login screen.

2. Supervisor enters email and password => clicks [Login].

3. System validates fields:
   3.1. Email empty => inline error: "Email is required." => does NOT authenticate.
   3.2. Password empty => inline error: "Password is required." => does NOT authenticate.
   3.3. Email format invalid => inline error: "Please enter a valid email address." => does NOT authenticate.

4. System checks failed attempt counter:
   4.1. >= 5 consecutive failed attempts within last 15 min => error: "Too many failed attempts. Please try again in 15 minutes." => does NOT authenticate.

5. System queries database:
   5.1. Email not found or role != MSP Supervisor => generic error: "Invalid email or password." => increments counter.
   5.2. Account Status = Inactive => error: "Your account is not active. Please contact the administrator." => does NOT increment counter.
   5.3. Account has no User Group assigned => error: "Your account has no scope assigned. Please contact the administrator." => does NOT increment counter.
   5.4. Password mismatch => generic error: "Invalid email or password." => increments counter.
   5.5. All valid:
    + Session token created and stored server-side.
    + Secure HTTP-only cookie set on client.
    + Failed attempt counter reset to 0.
    + Last login timestamp updated.
    + Navigates to Dashboard (5.1.6) scoped to Supervisor's User Group (buildings + asset systems).

Edge cases:
- Network error => error: "Unable to connect. Please check your connection and try again."
- Supervisor navigates to Login URL while already authenticated => redirected to Dashboard.
- Email pasted with leading/trailing spaces => trimmed before validation.
- Supervisor's User Group was deactivated after login => existing session continues but scoped data returns empty lists. On next login after session expiry, "no scope assigned" error shown until group is reactivated.

   5.X. Account Status = Pending (invitation not yet accepted) => does NOT create session => error: "Your account is not yet activated. Please check your email for the invitation link."

Description/Assumptions:
- Accessible by unauthenticated MSP Supervisors from Login screen.
- 2-screen flow: Email input + OTP request; OTP verification + new password.
- OTP sent to registered email via AWS SES/SendGrid.

- After reset => redirected to Login (5.1.1).

- Continue - Button - Primary - Full-width
- Back to Login - Hyperlink - Navigates to Login (5.1.1)

Screen 2 - Enter OTP and New Password

- Page title: "Reset Password"
- Instruction text: "Enter the verification code sent to [email address]."
- OTP Code - Text field - Required - Numeric - 6 digits
- Resend Code - Hyperlink - Active after 60 seconds

- Confirm New Password - Text field (masked) - Required - Must match

User Flow:
Screen: Forgot Password (MSP Supervisor)

Preconditions:
- No authentication required.
- Must have an active MSP Supervisor account with the provided email.

Screen 1 - Enter Email:
1. Supervisor clicks [Forgot Password?] on Login => Screen 1.
2. Enters email => clicks [Continue]:
   2.1. Empty => error: "Email is required."
   2.2. Format invalid => error: "Please enter a valid email address."
   2.3. Email not found in MSP Supervisor accounts => does NOT send OTP => error: "No account found with this email address."
   2.4. Account Inactive => does NOT send OTP => error: "Your account is not active. Please contact the administrator."
   2.5. Valid => OTP sent via AWS SES/SendGrid => navigates to Screen 2.
3. Clicks [Back to Login] => navigates to Login (5.1.1).

Screen 2 - Enter OTP & New Password:
4. Enters OTP / New Password / Confirm => clicks [Reset Password]:
   4.1. OTP empty => error: "Please enter the verification code."
   4.2. OTP non-numeric or wrong length => error: "Verification code must be 6 digits."
   4.3. OTP incorrect => error: "Invalid verification code." After 5 wrong attempts => OTP invalidated => must restart from Screen 1.
   4.4. OTP expired (> 10 min) => error: "This code has expired. Please request a new one." => returns to Screen 1.
   4.5. Password < 8 chars => error: "Password must be at least 8 characters."
   4.6. Confirm mismatch => error: "Passwords do not match."
   4.7. All valid:
    + Password updated in database.
    + All active sessions for this account immediately invalidated.
    + OTP consumed.
    + Navigates to Login (5.1.1).
    + Success toast on Login: "Password reset successfully. Please log in with your new password."
5. [Resend Code] (active after 60s): new OTP sent => previous invalidated.

Edge cases:
- Email delivery fails => error: "Failed to send verification code. Please try again later."
- All active sessions invalidated on reset => Supervisor must re-login on all devices.

User Flow:
Flow: Create MSP Technician Account (MSP Supervisor)

Preconditions:
- MSP Supervisor authenticated and belongs to a User Group.

2. Supervisor fills: Email / Full Name / optional fields.
   User Group is pre-filled (read-only) as Supervisor's own group.
3. Supervisor clicks [Create & Send Invitation]:
   3.1. Email empty => error: "Email is required."
   3.2. Email format invalid => error: "Please enter a valid email address."

- Supervisor's User Group changes after creating Technician => Technician remains in original group.

User Flow:
Flow: Deactivate MSP Technician Account (MSP Supervisor)

Preconditions:
- MSP Supervisor authenticated.
- Technician must be in Supervisor's own User Group.
- Technician must be in Active status.

1. Supervisor clicks Deactivate icon on Technician row.
2. Confirmation popup: "Are you sure you want to deactivate [Full Name]? They will no longer be able to log in."
3. Supervisor clicks [Confirm]:
   3.1. System sets Status => Inactive.
   3.2. All active sessions for this Technician invalidated immediately.
   3.3. Technician remains in User Group (not auto-removed). Group membership preserved.
   3.4. Active WOs assigned to this Technician: WOs remain in current status (not cancelled). Supervisor must reassign manually.

Reactivate (via Edit):
5. Supervisor clicks Edit on Inactive Technician => sets Status = Active => saves.
6. Technician can log in again. Scope restored from User Group.
7. Success: "MSP Technician account updated successfully."

Edge cases:
- Deactivated Technician has WO in Started status => WO continues but Technician cannot access app. Supervisor must handle manually (reassign or wait for next login after reactivation).
- Network error => "Failed to deactivate account. Please try again."

Description/Assumptions:
- Supervisor views all requests and WOs within User Group scope.
- Includes: Technician Requests (awaiting review) / own ad-hoc WOs / Service Request Accepted / all active WOs.

WO Status reference (per product document):
Group: Preparation => Draft
Group: New => Technician Request / Tenant Request / Service Request Accepted / Pending
Group: Pending => Unassigned (WO approved, ready for Technician assignment)
Group: In Progress => Assigned / Started
Group: Review => Completed (Supervisor reviews and signs off before forwarding to BM)
Group: Approval => Verified (BM reviews, signs, and approves to close)
Group: Finalized => Closed / Cancelled
Group: Rejection => Ad-hoc Declined / Approval Rejected / Completion Rejected / Verification Rejected
- View toggle: Table view (default) / Kanban view. Both views show the same data with the same filters applied. Kanban groups cards by Status Group (7 columns: New / Pending / In Progress / Review / Approval / Finalized / Rejected). View preference saved per user.

View toggle:
- [Table] - Button / Icon - Secondary - switches to table/list view

Kanban board (when Kanban view active):

- Column order: New | Pending | In Progress | Review | Approval | Finalized | Rejected
- Column header: Group label + card count badge (colored per group)
- Each card shows:
  + WO ID - monospace code - top-left
  + Specific status badge - top-right (e.g. Assigned, Started, Unassigned)
  + WO title / description (truncated to 2 lines)
  + Asset Code + Location

- Finalized column: shows latest 2-3 cards + collapsed "N more closed" row

Preconditions:

--- Approve ---

4. Confirmation popup: "Forward this request to Building Manager for approval?"

9. Supervisor enters reason => confirms:

Preconditions:
