User Flow:
Flow: Work Order Completion Review & Sign-off (MSP Supervisor)

Preconditions:
- MSP Supervisor authenticated.
- WO must be in Completed status (Technician submitted) or Verification Rejected status (BM returned).
- WO within Supervisor's User Group scope.

--- When WO is in Completed status ---

1. Supervisor opens WO Detail (5.3.3).
2. Supervisor systematically reviews the Execution tab:
   + Checklist: each item expanded to verify Description is filled and Photos uploaded per requirements (Required fields must have content).
   + Part Replacement table: verifies parts used match what was requested.
   + Sign-off section: confirms Technician A signed off / Building Technician signed off / Tenant (if applicable) signed off. Timestamps and names shown per signature.

3. Supervisor clicks [Sign Off & Forward to BM]:
   3.1. Confirmation popup: "Sign off and forward this work order to Building Manager for final review? Verify that all checklist items, photos, and signatures are complete before proceeding."
   3.2. Supervisor confirms:
    + Supervisor digital sign-off recorded with timestamp and Supervisor's name.
    + Status => Verified.
    + BM notified (in-app + email): "Work Order [ID] has been reviewed by MSP Supervisor and is ready for your final sign-off."
    + History log: "Signed off by [Supervisor Name] at [timestamp]."
    + Success toast: "Work order forwarded to Building Manager."
    + [Sign Off & Forward] and [Reject] buttons removed from page.
   3.3. Supervisor cancels => popup closes, no change.

4. Supervisor clicks [Reject]:
   4.1. Rejection popup opens with REQUIRED Rejection Reason field (max 500 chars).
   4.2. Reason empty => inline error: "Rejection reason is required." => does NOT reject.
   4.3. Supervisor enters specific reason (e.g., "Checklist item 3 is incomplete - missing photo evidence.") => confirms:
    + Status => Completion Rejected.
    + Technician notified (in-app + email): "Work Order [ID] has been rejected by Supervisor. Reason: [reason]. Please correct and resubmit."
    + History log: "Rejected by [Supervisor Name]. Reason: [reason] at [timestamp]."
    + Success toast: "Work order returned to Technician."
    + Action buttons removed.
   4.4. Cancels => popup closes, no change.

--- When WO is in Verification Rejected status (BM returned to Supervisor) ---

5. Supervisor opens WO Detail. A read-only banner at top of page shows:
   + "Rejected by [BM Name] on [date]. Reason: [BM's rejection reason]."

6. Supervisor reviews BM's rejection reason and reviews the execution content again.

7. CONFIRMED (per project document): When Verification Rejected, Supervisor CANNOT edit execution content (checklist, photos, parts). Supervisor must recheck the details and resubmit as-is with an optional note explaining what was reviewed. If the Supervisor believes corrections are needed, they must verbally coordinate with the Technician outside the system, then resubmit. There is no formal in-app workflow for Supervisor to send the WO back to Technician from Verification Rejected status.

8. Supervisor clicks [Review & Resubmit to BM]:
   8.1. Confirmation popup: "Confirm that you have reviewed the Building Manager's rejection reason and the work order is ready for resubmission."
   8.2. Supervisor confirms:
    + Status => Verified (again — re-enters Approval group for BM to review).
    + BM notified: "Work Order [ID] has been resubmitted by Supervisor for your review."
    + History log: "Resubmitted by [Supervisor Name] at [timestamp]. (After BM rejection.)"
    + Success toast: "Work order resubmitted to Building Manager."
   8.3. Cancels => no change.

Edge cases:
- BM rejects the same WO multiple times => each cycle creates new Verification Rejected history entry. Supervisor can resubmit multiple times.
- Supervisor signs off without thoroughly reviewing => system does not technically enforce a review period; this is a process responsibility.
- Network error on sign-off => "Failed to sign off. Please try again." => WO remains in Completed status.

   + Red border shown if submitted empty.
5. Supervisor enters specific, actionable reason => clicks [Reject]:

   5.2. Status changed concurrently => error: "Work order status has changed. Please refresh."
   5.3. Valid:
    + Status => Completion Rejected.
    + Technician notified (in-app + email): "Work Order [ID] has been rejected. Please correct and resubmit. Reason: [reason]."

- User Group must have at least 1 building and 1 asset system.

1. Supervisor clicks [+ Create Plan] => Create Maintenance Plan page.
2. Supervisor fills required fields:
   - Plan Name (required, max 150 chars).
   - Asset System (required, dropdown restricted to Supervisor's User Group systems).
   - Asset Sub-system (required, cascades from System).
   - Asset Type (required, cascades from Sub-system).
   - Building (required, restricted to Supervisor's User Group buildings).

3. Optional: Description / Remark / Photos.

   4.1. Plan Name empty => error: "Plan name is required." => does NOT create.
   4.2. Plan Name > 150 chars => error: "Plan name must not exceed 150 characters."
   4.3. Asset System / Sub-system / Asset Type not selected => respective errors.
   4.4. Building not selected => error: "Building is required."
   4.5. Building outside Supervisor's scope => not in dropdown.
   4.6. Frequency not selected => error: "Frequency is required."
   4.7. Time Required empty => error: "Time required is required."
   4.8. All valid:
    + Plan created with Status: Pending (NOT Active — Supervisor plans require BM approval).
    + Plan ID auto-generated.
    + BM notified (in-app + email): "A new maintenance plan from Supervisor [Name] requires your approval."
    + History log: "Plan submitted by [Supervisor Name] at [timestamp]. Status: Pending."
    + Success toast: "Maintenance plan submitted for Building Manager approval."
    + Navigates back to Plan List.
5. Supervisor clicks [Cancel] => navigates back without saving.

After BM approval:
6. Plan status => Active. WO auto-generation begins on next cycle.
7. Supervisor receives notification: "Your maintenance plan [Name] has been approved by Building Manager."

After BM rejection:
8. Plan status => Approval Rejected.
9. Supervisor receives notification: "Your maintenance plan [Name] has been rejected. Reason: [reason]. Please revise and resubmit."
10. Supervisor opens the plan => clicks [Edit Plan] to revise => resubmits.

Edge cases:
- Supervisor submits identical plan (same Name + Building + Asset Type + Frequency) that already exists => system does NOT block (no uniqueness constraint on plan content). Flagged for BA review.
- Network error => "Failed to create plan. Please try again." => no partial record.

    + History log updated: "Plan edited by [Supervisor Name] at [timestamp]."
    + Success toast: "Plan updated successfully."
4. Supervisor clicks [Cancel] => popup closes, no change.

Resubmit after BM rejection:
5. Supervisor edits the rejected plan.
6. Supervisor clicks [Resubmit to BM] (or [Submit for BM Approval] becomes available again):
   + Status => Pending (re-enters BM approval queue).
   + BM notified: "Maintenance plan [Name] has been revised and resubmitted for your approval."
   + History log: "Resubmitted by [Supervisor Name] at [timestamp]."

Edge cases:
- Supervisor tries to edit an Active plan => Edit icon not shown in list; [Edit Plan] button not shown in Detail. Supervisor must contact Admin/BM to modify.
- Network error => "Failed to update plan. Please try again."

Description/Assumptions:
- Accessible by MSP Supervisor on mobile (Android & iOS).
- Account created by Super Admin or Building Manager via web portal.
- Supervisor MUST belong to a User Group. Scope defined by User Group: buildings + asset systems.
- On successful login => Dashboard (6.1.5) scoped to Supervisor's User Group.
- If valid session exists => system skips Login, navigates to Dashboard.
- Maximum 5 consecutive failed attempts before 15-minute lockout.
- All connections use HTTPS/SSL with TLS 1.2+.
Fields/Components, Component Type, Requi:
Screen: Login (MSP Supervisor Mobile)

- EZAxis logo - Display - Centered top
- Email - Text field - Required - Keyboard type: email - Placeholder: "Enter your email" - Max 100 characters
- Password - Text field (masked) - Required - Placeholder: "Enter your password" - Min 8 characters - Show/hide toggle
- Forgot Password? - Hyperlink - Navigates to Forgot Password (6.1.3)
- Login - Button - Primary - Full-width - Large tap target
- Loading indicator while authenticating
User Flow:
Screen: Login (MSP Supervisor Mobile)

Preconditions:
- No authentication required - entry point for unauthenticated MSP Supervisor on mobile.
- Account must exist with role = MSP Supervisor AND must belong to a User Group.
- If valid session token stored on device => system skips Login and navigates to Dashboard.

1. App opens. System checks for stored session token on device:
   1.1. Valid token => navigates to Dashboard (6.1.5) scoped to User Group => flow ends.
   1.2. No token or expired => renders Login screen.

2. Supervisor enters email and password => taps [Login].
   + Loading spinner shown on [Login] while authenticating.

3. System validates fields:
   3.1. Email empty => inline error: "Email is required."
   3.2. Password empty => inline error: "Password is required."
   3.3. Email format invalid => inline error: "Please enter a valid email address."

4. System checks failed attempt counter:
   4.1. >= 5 failed attempts within 15 min => error toast: "Too many failed attempts. Please try again in 15 minutes."

5. System queries backend:
   5.1. Email not found or role != MSP Supervisor => error toast: "Invalid email or password." => increments counter.
   5.2. Account Inactive => error toast: "Your account is not active. Please contact the administrator."
   5.3. Account has no User Group => error toast: "Your account has no scope assigned. Please contact the administrator."
   5.4. Password mismatch => error toast: "Invalid email or password." => increments counter.
   5.5. All valid:
    + Session token stored securely on device (Keychain/Keystore).
    + Failed attempt counter reset.
    + Last login timestamp updated.
    + Navigates to Dashboard (6.1.5) scoped to Supervisor's User Group.

Edge cases:
- Network unavailable when tapping [Login] => error toast: "No internet connection. Please check your network and try again."
- Token on device is expired (server-side) => system detects on first API call => redirects to Login: "Your session has expired. Please log in again."
- Supervisor's User Group deactivated after login => session continues but API returns empty scoped data.
- Email pasted with spaces => trimmed before validation.

   5.X. Account Status = Pending (invitation not yet accepted) => does NOT create session => error: "Your account is not yet activated. Please check your email for the invitation link."

Description/Assumptions:
- Accessible by authenticated MSP Supervisor from any screen on mobile.
- Accessible from Profile/More in bottom navigation.
- Native alert confirmation before logout.
- On logout: session token invalidated and removed from device.
Fields/Components, Component Type, Requi:
Component: Logout (MSP Supervisor Mobile)

Access path: Bottom navigation => Profile/More => Sign out

- Sign out - Menu item

Alert: Confirm Sign Out
- Message: "Are you sure you want to sign out?"
- Sign out - Button - Destructive
- Cancel - Button - Default
User Flow:
Flow: Logout (MSP Supervisor Mobile)

Preconditions:
- MSP Supervisor authenticated on mobile.

1. Supervisor taps Profile or More icon in bottom navigation.
2. Profile/Settings screen opens. Supervisor taps [Sign out].
3. Native OS alert:
   + Title: "Sign Out"
   + Message: "Are you sure you want to sign out?"
   + [Sign out] (destructive) | [Cancel].
4a. Supervisor taps [Sign out]:
   + Session token invalidated server-side.
   + Token removed from device secure storage.
   + Any cached data cleared from app local storage.
   + FCM push token unregistered (no more notifications until next login).
   + App navigates to Login screen (6.1.1).
4b. Supervisor taps [Cancel]:
   + Alert dismisses. Supervisor remains authenticated.

Edge cases:
- Network unavailable during logout => local token removed and app navigates to Login. Server-side invalidation retried when online or expires naturally.
- App force-closed and reopened => session token re-checked on app open (step 1).
- Session expires while app in background => on next foreground API call, 401 detected => clears local token => navigates to Login: "Your session has expired."

User Flow:
Screen: Forgot Password (MSP Supervisor Mobile)

Preconditions:
- No authentication required. Must have an active MSP Supervisor account.

Screen 1 - Enter Email:
1. Supervisor taps [Forgot Password?] on Login => Screen 1.
2. Enters email (keyboard type: email) => taps [Continue]:
   2.1. Empty => error: "Email is required."
   2.2. Format invalid => error: "Please enter a valid email address."
   2.3. Not found in MSP Supervisor accounts => error: "No account found with this email address."
   2.4. Account Inactive => error: "Your account is not active. Please contact the administrator."
   2.5. Network unavailable => error: "No internet connection."
   2.6. Valid => OTP sent via AWS SES/SendGrid => navigates to Screen 2.
3. Taps [Back to Login] => navigates to Login (6.1.1).

Screen 2 - Enter OTP & New Password:
4. Supervisor enters OTP (numeric keyboard, 6 digits) / New Password / Confirm.
5. Taps [Reset Password]:
   5.1. OTP empty => error: "Please enter the verification code."
   5.2. Wrong format => error: "Verification code must be 6 digits."
   5.3. OTP incorrect => error: "Invalid verification code." After 5 wrong attempts => OTP invalidated => restart from Screen 1.
   5.4. OTP expired => error: "This code has expired. Please request a new one." => returns to Screen 1.
   5.5. Password < 8 chars => error: "Password must be at least 8 characters."
   5.6. Confirm mismatch => error: "Passwords do not match."
   5.7. Network unavailable => error: "No internet connection."
   5.8. All valid:
    + Password updated. All active sessions invalidated.
    + Navigates to Login (6.1.1).
    + Success toast: "Password reset successfully. Please log in with your new password."
6. [Resend Code] (active after 60s with countdown timer): new OTP sent => previous invalidated.

Edge cases:
- Email delivery fails => "Failed to send verification code. Please try again later."
- App closed mid-reset => returns to Login screen (Screen 2 state not persisted).

   8.4. Selected Technician became inactive => error toast: "Selected Technician is no longer active. Please choose another."

    + History log updated.
    + Success toast: "Work order assigned to [Technician Name]."
    + Bottom sheet closes. WO Detail refreshes with new status and assigned Technician shown.

9. Supervisor taps [Cancel] or drags sheet down => dismisses, no change.

6. Supervisor reviews BM's feedback.
7. CONFIRMED: Supervisor CANNOT edit execution content after Verification Rejected. Supervisor can only add an optional note before resubmitting as-is.

Description/Assumptions:
- FCM delivery to MSP Supervisor mobile devices.
- Trigger events: New Technician Request / WO Ready to Assign / Verification Rejected / Spare Part events / Maintenance Plan Approved/Rejected.
- Online: immediate. Offline: FCM queues until reconnect (max 28 days).
- Logout unregisters FCM token.
Fields/Components, Component Type, Requi:
Push notification (device OS) - System-triggered automation - No dedicated UI screen rendered.

Notification structure per event:
- Title: short label (e.g., "New Technician Request")
- Body: descriptive message with WO ID and Building
- Action: deep link to relevant screen on tap

In-app bell icon shows unread notification count badge.
User Flow:
System Process: Push Notification (MSP Supervisor Mobile)

--- Delivery mechanism ---
1. System event triggers notification for MSP Supervisor.
2. Backend sends payload to Firebase Cloud Messaging (FCM).
3. FCM routes to all registered devices for this Supervisor account.
4. Online device => delivered immediately.
5. Offline device => FCM queues; delivered when device comes online (expire after 28 days).

--- Trigger events ---

New Technician Ad-hoc Request (status = Technician Request):
Title: "New Technician Request"
Body: "Technician [Name] submitted an ad-hoc work order request. Tap to review."
Action: opens WO Detail (6.2.3).

WO Ready to Assign (BM approved, status = Pending - Unassigned):
Title: "Work Order Ready for Assignment"
Body: "Work Order [ID] for [Building Name] is approved and ready for Technician assignment."
Action: opens WO Detail (6.2.3).

Verification Rejected by BM (BM returned Verified WO to Supervisor):
Title: "Work Order Returned by Building Manager"
Body: "Work Order [ID] rejected by [BM Name]. Please review and resubmit."
Action: opens WO Detail (6.2.3) showing Verification Rejected banner.

Spare Part Request Approved by BM:
Title: "Spare Part Request Approved"
Body: "Building Manager approved the spare part request for Work Order [ID]."
Action: opens WO Detail (6.2.3).

Spare Part Request Rejected by BM:
Title: "Spare Part Request Rejected"
Body: "Building Manager rejected the spare part request for Work Order [ID]."
Action: opens WO Detail (6.2.3).

Spare Part Unavailable:
Title: "Spare Part Unavailable"
Body: "Spare part [Name] is unavailable for Work Order [ID]. BM has been notified."
Action: opens WO Detail (6.2.3).

Stock Available (after restock resolves Unavailable):
Title: "Spare Part Now Available"
Body: "Spare part [Name] is now available for Work Order [ID]."
Action: opens WO Detail (6.2.3).

Maintenance Plan Approved by BM:
Title: "Maintenance Plan Approved"
Body: "Your maintenance plan [Name] has been approved. WOs will be generated on the next cycle."
Action: navigates user to log in to web portal (no plan management on mobile).

Maintenance Plan Rejected by BM:
Title: "Maintenance Plan Rejected"
Body: "Your maintenance plan [Name] was rejected. Reason: [reason]. Please revise and resubmit."
Action: navigates user to log in to web portal.

--- In-app notification handling ---
6. App open (foreground): in-app banner at top, auto-dismisses after 4 seconds, tap to navigate.
7. App in background: OS notification in system tray, badge count increments.
8. App closed: OS notification shown, tap to cold-start app and navigate.
9. Notification bell icon shows unread badge count.
10. Tapping notification navigates to relevant screen and marks as read.

--- Permission and lifecycle ---
11. First install: OS requests notification permission.
12. If denied => no push notifications. In-app prompt to enable in device Settings.
13. Logout => FCM token unregistered => no notifications until next login.
14. App reinstall => FCM token regenerated => re-registered on next login.

Edge cases:
- Notification for a WO already processed => tapping shows current (processed) status.
- Multiple notifications received offline => batched delivery on reconnect.
- FCM token expires or changes => refreshed on next app open.

Description/Assumptions:
- Accessible by MSP Technician on mobile (Android & iOS).
- Account created by Super Admin, Building Manager, or MSP Supervisor.
- Technician MUST belong to a User Group. Scope defined by User Group: buildings + asset systems.
- On successful login => Dashboard (7.1.5) showing only Technician's own assigned WOs.
- If valid session exists => system skips Login, navigates to Dashboard.
- Maximum 5 consecutive failed attempts before 15-minute lockout.
- All connections use HTTPS/SSL with TLS 1.2+.
Fields/Components, Component Type, Requi:
Screen: Login (MSP Technician Mobile)

- EZAxis logo - Display - Centered top
- Email - Text field - Required - Keyboard type: email - Placeholder: "Enter your email" - Max 100 characters
- Password - Text field (masked) - Required - Placeholder: "Enter your password" - Min 8 characters - Show/hide toggle
- Forgot Password? - Hyperlink - Navigates to Forgot Password (7.1.3)
- Login - Button - Primary - Full-width - Large tap target
- Loading indicator while authenticating
User Flow:
Screen: Login (MSP Technician Mobile)

Preconditions:
- No authentication required - entry point for unauthenticated MSP Technician on mobile.
- Account must exist with role = MSP Technician AND must belong to a User Group.
- If valid session token stored on device => system skips Login and navigates to Dashboard.

1. App opens. System checks for stored session token:
   1.1. Valid token => navigates to Dashboard (7.1.5) showing only this Technician's WOs => flow ends.
   1.2. No token or expired => renders Login screen.

2. Technician enters email and password => taps [Login].
   + Loading spinner shown while authenticating.

3. System validates fields:
   3.1. Email empty => inline error: "Email is required."
   3.2. Password empty => inline error: "Password is required."
   3.3. Email format invalid => inline error: "Please enter a valid email address."

4. System checks failed attempt counter:
   4.1. >= 5 failed attempts within 15 min => error toast: "Too many failed attempts. Please try again in 15 minutes."

5. System queries backend:
   5.1. Email not found or role != MSP Technician => error toast: "Invalid email or password." => increments counter.
   5.2. Account Inactive => error toast: "Your account is not active. Please contact your Supervisor."
   5.3. Account has no User Group => error toast: "Your account has no scope assigned. Please contact your Supervisor."
   5.4. Password mismatch => error toast: "Invalid email or password." => increments counter.
   5.5. All valid:
    + Session token stored securely on device (Keychain/Keystore).
    + Failed attempt counter reset.
    + Last login timestamp updated.
    + Navigates to Dashboard (7.1.5).

Edge cases:
- Network unavailable => error toast: "No internet connection. Please check your network and try again."
- Technician's User Group deactivated => login succeeds but app shows empty scope data.
- Token expired server-side => detected on first API call => redirects to Login: "Your session has expired. Please log in again."
- Error message says "contact your Supervisor" (not "administrator") because Technician's point of contact is their Supervisor.

   5.X. Account Status = Pending (invitation not yet accepted) => does NOT create session => error: "Your account is not yet activated. Please check your email for the invitation link."

User Flow:
Screen: Offline Mode (MSP Technician Mobile)

Preconditions:
- MSP Technician authenticated.
- Device has no internet connection.

1. App detects loss of internet connectivity:
   + Persistent offline banner appears at top of all screens: "You are offline. Some features are unavailable."
   + Banner color: amber/yellow to distinguish from critical errors.

2. What is available offline:
   + View last cached WO list (read-only - as of last sync).
   + View last cached WO details (checklist, asset info, location).
   + View last cached asset list and asset details.
   + View last cached spare part list (for reference).
   + Cache TTL: assumed 24 hours (pending client confirmation - flagged for BA review).

3. What is NOT available offline (all write operations and new data fetches):
   + Starting a WO (requires server-side status change).
   + Submitting checklist updates.
   + Uploading photos.
   + Submitting sign-off.
   + Creating ad-hoc requests.
   + Requesting spare parts.
   + Logging out (server-side session invalidation unavailable - see edge case).

4. Technician attempts a disabled action (e.g., taps [Start Work Order]):
   + Error toast: "You are offline. Please reconnect to perform this action."
   + Action does NOT proceed.

5. App detects internet reconnection:
   + Offline banner disappears (or changes to green "Back online" for 3 seconds then dismisses).
   + All cached data auto-refreshes in background.
   + Pending actions (if any queued) are synced automatically.
   + All action buttons re-enabled.

6. App is closed while offline and reopened while still offline:
   + Cached data is displayed. Offline banner shown.
   + Last sync timestamp shown in a subtle label: "Last synced: [time]."

Edge cases:
- Technician is mid-checklist fill when connection drops => data typed into form fields is preserved in app memory. Fields are read-only or save-to-local-draft until reconnect. On reconnect, Technician can resume and submit. (Local draft persistence is pending client confirmation - flagged for BA review.)
- Cache TTL expired (> 24 hours offline) => app shows: "Cached data has expired. Please reconnect to reload your work orders."
- Technician tries to logout while offline => local token and cache cleared; app navigates to Login. Server-side invalidation occurs when device reconnects.
- Photo upload queued offline (if offline queue is implemented) => photos uploaded automatically on reconnect. If not implemented in MVP => Technician must retake and upload after reconnect.

Preconditions:
- MSP Technician authenticated.

1. Technician taps [+ New Request] or [Create Ad-hoc WO] in bottom navigation or WO list.
2. Create Ad-hoc Request screen loads.

3. Technician fills required fields:

   - Asset Sub-system (optional, cascades).
   - Asset Type (optional, cascades).
   - Asset (optional, searchable or [Scan QR] to auto-fill):
     + Taps [Scan QR] => camera opens => scans asset QR.
     + If asset in scope => Asset, Building, Floor, Area auto-filled.
     + If asset outside scope => error toast: "This asset is outside your scope."

   - Floor (optional, cascades from Building).
   - Area/Unit (optional).

4. Technician taps [Submit]:
   4.1. Asset System not selected => error: "Asset System is required."
   4.2. Building not selected => error: "Building is required."
   4.3. Description empty => error: "Description is required."

   4.5. Attachment invalid type => error: "Only JPG, PNG, and PDF files are accepted."

Preconditions:

3. Technician taps a checklist item card => card expands showing:
   + Item name (read-only, inherited from Asset Type definition).
   + Description field (if setting = Required or Optional):
    - Text area with placeholder: "Enter description..."
    - If Required: red asterisk shown. Must be filled before submission.
    - If Optional: no asterisk. Can be left empty.
    - If Off: field not shown.
   + Photos section (if setting = Required or Optional):

3. Bottom sheet slides up with options:

   + [Choose from Library] (gallery icon) — opens photo gallery.
   + [Cancel] — dismisses sheet.

--- Camera path ---
4a. Technician taps [Take Photo]:
   4a.1. Camera permission not granted => OS permission dialog appears. If denied => error: "Camera permission required. Please enable in device Settings => App => Camera."
   4a.2. Camera opens. Technician frames the evidence and taps capture.
   4a.3. Preview screen shows the captured photo.
   4a.4. Technician taps [Use Photo] => photo added to checklist item's photo grid. Thumbnail appears.
   4a.5. Technician taps [Retake] => camera reopens.

--- Gallery path ---
4b. Technician taps [Choose from Library]:

   4b.2. Photo library opens. Technician selects a photo.

--- After photo added ---
5. Photo uploaded to server in background (while Technician continues with other checklist items):

- Photo taken in offline state => stored locally. Uploaded automatically on reconnect.
- Device camera malfunctions => OS error returned; app shows: "Unable to access camera. Please try again."

- Note: This is different from Part Replacement recording (7.2.8). This is a formal request to BM for stock-out approval.

1. Technician opens WO Detail => Execution tab => taps [Request Spare Parts].
2. Bottom sheet slides up: "Request Spare Parts"
   + Multiple part entries supported via [+ Add another part].
   + Per entry:
    - Spare Part: searchable dropdown (User Group scope, Active only). Shows: Name / Code / Available Stock.
    - Quantity: numeric input, min 1.
   + Notes: optional textarea.

3. Technician taps [Submit Request]:
   3.1. Spare Part not selected => error: "Please select a spare part."
   3.2. Quantity < 1 => error: "Please enter a valid quantity."
   3.3. Duplicate part entries => warning: "Duplicate part detected. Please combine into one entry."
   3.4. Requested qty > Available stock => warning (not blocked): "Requested quantity exceeds available stock. This request will be flagged as Unavailable." => Technician confirms.
   3.5. Network unavailable => error toast: "No internet connection."
   3.6. All valid:
    + For each part: If stock sufficient => On-Hold set immediately. If insufficient => flagged Unavailable.
    + BM notified (FCM push + email): "Spare part request for Work Order [ID] from Technician [Name] requires your approval."
    + Supervisor notified (FCM push): "Technician [Name] submitted a spare part request for Work Order [ID]."
    + History log updated.
    + Success toast: "Spare parts request submitted for Building Manager approval."
    + Bottom sheet closes.

4. Taps [Cancel] or drags sheet down => dismisses, no request created.

Edge cases:
- Technician submits request and BM declines => On-Hold released. Technician notified. Technician must decide whether to use Purchase Separately recording instead.
- Spare Part dropdown empty => "No spare parts available for your scope."
- Network times out => no request created; Technician can retry.

Description/Assumptions:
- CONFIRMED from WO Execution diagram: 3-step sign-off after checklist completion.
- Step 1: MSP Technician A (this Technician) - always required.
- Step 2: Building Technician (another MSP Tech) - always required.
- Step 3: Tenant sign-off - configurable per plan (on/off).
- Sign-off METHOD: PENDING confirmation (flagged for BA review).
- After all sign-offs => Set Completed => status: Completed.
Fields/Components, Component Type, Requi:
Embedded in WO Execution (triggered after [Submit & Sign Off]):

Step 1 - Technician self sign-off:
- System auto-records this Technician's sign-off + timestamp
- Proceeds to Step 2 automatically

Step 2 - Building Technician sign-off:
- Prompt: Present device to Building Technician
- Sign-off input - Method TBC (signature capture / confirm button)
- [Confirm Sign-off] - Primary

Step 3 - Tenant sign-off (if configured):
- Prompt: Present device to Tenant
- Sign-off input - Method TBC
- [Confirm Sign-off] - Primary
- Skip if Tenant sign-off not configured

After all sign-offs:
- System sets status: Completed
- Supervisor notified: Work Order [ID] completed - ready for your review
User Flow:
Flow: Technician Sign-off & Submit Work Order (MSP Technician Mobile)

Preconditions:
- MSP Technician authenticated. WO in Started status. Main Technician.
- All required checklist items must be completed before submission is allowed.
- All required Description fields filled and all required Photos uploaded.

This is triggered by tapping [Submit & Sign Off] on WO Detail (button is disabled until all Required items are complete).

1. Technician ensures all checklist items are done. Progress indicator shows "5 of 5 items done."
2. [Submit & Sign Off] button becomes enabled (no longer grayed out).
3. Technician taps [Submit & Sign Off].

4. System validates ALL required fields (final validation before sign-off process begins):
   4.1. Any Required Description field empty on any checklist item => inline validation:
    + Checklist section scrolls to first incomplete item.
    + Red highlight on incomplete item.
    + Error toast: "Please complete all required checklist items before submitting."
    + [Submit & Sign Off] remains disabled.
   4.2. Any Required Photos section with 0 photos on any checklist item:
    + Same behavior: scroll to first incomplete item, highlight, error toast.
   4.3. All required fields complete => proceeds to Sign-off sequence.

5. Sign-off sequence begins (3 steps in order):

   STEP 1: MSP Technician A (this Technician) — ALWAYS REQUIRED
   + Signature screen or confirmation screen shown:
    - Title: "Your Sign-off"
    - Message: "Sign off as MSP Technician A to confirm work completion."
    - Sign-off method: ASSUMED = Button confirmation (tap [Sign Off] on device). Each party physically holds the device and taps to confirm. No signature capture or OTP in MVP.
   + System records: Technician name + timestamp.
   + Proceeds to Step 2.

   STEP 2: Building Technician — ALWAYS REQUIRED
   + Title: "Building Technician Sign-off"
   + Message: "Have the Building Technician sign off to witness completion."
   + Another MSP Technician (not this Technician) must confirm.
   + Sign-off method: ASSUMED = Button confirmation. Building Technician holds the device and taps [Confirm] to witness completion.
   + System records: Building Technician name/ID + timestamp.
   + If Building Technician is not available: Supervisor may override (pending BA confirmation — flagged).
   + Proceeds to Step 3.

   STEP 3: Tenant — CONDITIONAL (based on Maintenance Plan configuration)
   + If plan has "Tenant sign-off required = Yes":
    - Title: "Tenant Sign-off"
    - Message: "Have the Tenant confirm work completion."
    - Sign-off method: ASSUMED = Button confirmation. Tenant holds the device and taps [Confirm] to acknowledge work completion.
    - System records: Tenant name/ID + timestamp.
   + If plan has "Tenant sign-off required = No" (or WO is Ad-hoc, not from plan):
    - Step 3 is skipped automatically.

6. After all required sign-offs collected:
   + WO status => Completed.
   + MSP Supervisor notified (FCM push + email): "Technician [Name] has completed Work Order [ID] and submitted for your review."
   + History log: "Completed and signed off by [Technician Name] at [timestamp]. Sign-offs: [list]."
   + Success toast: "Work order submitted for Supervisor review."
   + Action buttons removed. WO Detail shows Completed status.

Edge cases:
- Building Technician not available on-site => pending client confirmation on how to handle. Flagged for BA review.
- Any step's sign-off fails (network error) => error toast: "Failed to record sign-off. Please try again." => sign-off progress preserved (already completed steps not lost).
- Technician accidentaly taps wrong button on sign-off screen => no undo after each individual sign-off step is confirmed (pending BA confirmation).
- Sign-off method ASSUMED = Button confirmation for all 3 parties. No signature drawing or OTP required in MVP. Each party physically taps [Sign Off] / [Confirm] on the Technician's device.

Description/Assumptions:
- WO returned with status: Completion Rejected.
- Technician reviews rejection reason => corrects checklist/photos/parts => resubmits.
- PENDING: whether checklist is preserved or reset after rejection.
- Note: Resubmit is not a destructive action. No confirmation popup required - [Review & Resubmit] proceeds directly after validation.
Fields/Components, Component Type, Requi:
Screen: WO Detail (status = Completion Rejected)
- Rejection details section (read-only):
  + Rejected by / Rejected time / Rejection Reason
- [Review & Resubmit] - Button - Primary
- Checklist items available for correction (7.2.6)
- Photos can be updated (7.2.7)
- Part Replacement can be updated (7.2.8)
User Flow:
Flow: Resubmit Work Order after Completion Rejected (MSP Technician Mobile)

Preconditions:
- MSP Technician authenticated.
- WO must be in Completion Rejected status.
- Technician must be the Main Technician.

1. Technician opens WO Detail (7.2.4). Red banner at top shows:
   + "Rejected by Supervisor [Name] on [date]. Reason: [reason]."
2. Technician reads the rejection reason carefully.

3. Technician reviews execution content and makes corrections:

   If checklist items need correction:
   3a. Technician swipes to Execution tab.
   3b. Taps on the relevant checklist item(s) to expand.
   3c. Updates Description text and/or replaces/adds Photos.
   3d. Checklist progress indicator shows current completion status.

   If part replacement entries need correction:
   3e. Technician removes incorrect entries and/or adds new entries.

   Note: Whether checklist progress is PRESERVED (Technician corrects specific items) or RESET after rejection is PENDING client confirmation. Current assumption: preserved. Flagged for BA review.

4. Technician taps [Review & Resubmit] at bottom of screen (Primary, full-width).

5. System runs the same final validation as Submit & Sign Off (7.2.10 step 4):
   5.1. Any Required Description empty => error toast: "Please complete all required checklist items." Scrolls to first incomplete item.
   5.2. Any Required Photos missing => same error.
   5.3. All valid => proceeds to sign-off sequence.

6. Sign-off sequence restarts from Step 1 (same 3-step process as 7.2.10 step 5):
   + All previous sign-offs are cleared. A fresh sign-off is required.
   + Reason: work has been modified; all parties must re-confirm the corrected version.

7. After all sign-offs collected:
   + WO status => Completed (re-submitted).
   + MSP Supervisor notified: "Technician [Name] has resubmitted Work Order [ID] after rejection."
   + History log: "Resubmitted by [Technician Name] at [timestamp]. (After rejection.)"
   + Success toast: "Work order resubmitted for Supervisor review."

Edge cases:
- Technician resubmits without making any changes to the rejected items => system does not block resubmission (validation only checks required field completion, not change detection). It is the Supervisor's responsibility to reject again if corrections are insufficient.
- Technician's checklist was reset (if that is the confirmed behavior) => Technician must re-complete all items from scratch.
- Network error during resubmission => "Failed to resubmit. Please try again." => WO remains in Completion Rejected.

Description/Assumptions:
- Technician scans asset QR code to quickly navigate to asset context.
- QR code printed on physical asset.
Fields/Components, Component Type, Requi:
Feature: QR Scanner (accessible from Asset List and WO Create form)
- [Scan QR] - Icon button - Opens device camera
- Camera overlay with scan frame
User Flow:
Flow: Scan Asset QR Code (MSP Technician Mobile)

Preconditions:
- MSP Technician authenticated.
- Camera permission must be granted.

1. Technician taps [Scan QR] icon (accessible from Asset List header, WO Detail, or Create WO screen).
2. Camera opens with a scan frame overlay.
   + Scan frame highlights the target area for the QR code.
   + Flash toggle icon for low-light conditions.
   + [Cancel] button to exit without scanning.

3. Technician points camera at the asset QR code:
   3.1. QR code decoded successfully:
    + System looks up the asset by the encoded asset URL/ID.

   3.2. Asset found AND asset is within Technician's scope AND active WO exists for this asset (Assigned or Started, assigned to this Technician):
    + System navigates directly to WO Detail (7.2.4) for that WO.
    + Toast: "Work order found for [Asset Name]."

   3.3. Asset found AND in scope AND no active WO exists:
    + System navigates to Asset Detail (7.3.2).
    + Toast: "No active work order for this asset."

   3.4. Asset found BUT outside Technician's scope (wrong building or system):
    + Does NOT navigate to asset.
    + Error toast: "This asset is outside your scope."

   3.5. Asset not found (QR code does not match any asset in system):
    + Error toast: "Asset not found. Please check the QR code and try again."

   3.6. QR code detected but cannot be decoded (corrupted, wrong format):
    + Error toast: "Unable to read this QR code. Please try again or search manually."

4. Camera fails to detect QR code within 30 seconds:
   + Screen shows hint: "Having trouble? Make sure the QR code is well-lit and centered in the frame."

5. Technician taps [Cancel] => camera closes, returns to previous screen.

Edge cases:
- Low light => Technician taps flash icon to turn on torch.
- Multiple QR codes in frame => system reads the largest/clearest one; if ambiguous, prompts Technician to adjust.
- QR code printed at an angle or on a curved surface => may require Technician to adjust angle for reliable scanning.
- Camera permission not granted => error: "Camera permission required. Please enable in device Settings => App => Camera." => navigates to Settings or shows manual search as fallback.

Description/Assumptions:
- FCM delivery to MSP Technician mobile devices.
- 11 trigger events: WO Assigned / WO from Plan / Added as Sub Tech / Completion Rejected / Ad-hoc Declined / Spare Part Approved / Rejected / Unavailable / WO Due Today / WO Overdue / WO Closed.
- Online: immediate. Offline: FCM queues until reconnect (max 28 days).
- Logout unregisters FCM token. Reinstall regenerates token.
Fields/Components, Component Type, Requi:
Push notification (device OS) - System-triggered - No dedicated UI screen.

Notification structure:
- Title: short label
- Body: descriptive message with WO ID / Part name
- Action: deep link to relevant detail screen on tap

In-app: bell icon with unread badge count.
Foreground: in-app banner, auto-dismisses after 4 seconds.
Background: OS system tray, badge count increments.
Closed: OS notification, cold-start app on tap.
User Flow:
System Process: Push Notification (MSP Technician Mobile)

--- Delivery mechanism ---
1. Backend sends payload to FCM for this Technician's account.
2. Online => immediate. Offline => queued up to 28 days.

--- Trigger events ---

WO Assigned (Supervisor assigned, status = Assigned):
Title: "New Work Order Assigned"
Body: "Work Order [ID] for [Asset Name] at [Building] has been assigned to you."
Action: opens WO Detail (7.2.4).

WO from Maintenance Plan (auto-generated, pre-assigned):
Title: "New Maintenance Work Order"
Body: "Maintenance work order [ID] for [Asset Name] has been assigned to you."
Action: opens WO Detail (7.2.4).

Added as Sub Technician:
Title: "Added to Work Order"
Body: "You have been added as Sub Technician on Work Order [ID]."
Action: opens WO Detail (7.2.4).

Completion Rejected:
Title: "Work Order Rejected"
Body: "Work Order [ID] rejected by Supervisor. Please review and resubmit."
Action: opens WO Detail (7.2.4) with Completion Rejected banner.

Ad-hoc Request Declined:
Title: "Ad-hoc Request Declined"
Body: "Your ad-hoc request was declined. Tap to view and resubmit."
Action: opens Ad-hoc Request List (7.2.2).

Spare Part Request Approved by BM:
Title: "Spare Part Request Approved"
Body: "BM approved spare part request for Work Order [ID]."
Action: opens WO Detail (7.2.4).

Spare Part Request Rejected by BM:
Title: "Spare Part Request Rejected"
Body: "BM rejected spare part request for Work Order [ID]."
Action: opens WO Detail (7.2.4).

Spare Part Unavailable:
Title: "Spare Part Unavailable"
Body: "Requested spare part for Work Order [ID] is unavailable. BM notified."
Action: opens WO Detail (7.2.4).

WO Due Today:
Title: "Work Order Due Today"
Body: "Work Order [ID] is due today. Please start as soon as possible."
Action: opens WO Detail (7.2.4).

WO Overdue:
Title: "Work Order Overdue"
Body: "Work Order [ID] is overdue. Please complete and submit."
Action: opens WO Detail (7.2.4).

WO Closed (BM final sign-off):
Title: "Work Order Closed"
Body: "Work Order [ID] has been officially closed."
Action: opens WO Detail (7.2.4).

--- In-app handling ---
Foreground: in-app banner auto-dismisses after 4 seconds.
Background: OS tray, badge count increments.
Closed: cold-start app and navigate to detail screen.

--- Permissions ---
First install: OS requests permission.
Denied => no push notifications; in-app prompt to enable in Settings.
Logout => FCM token unregistered.
Reinstall => token regenerated on next login.

Edge cases:
- Notification for already-processed WO => shows current status.
- Multiple offline notifications => batched delivery on reconnect.
- FCM token changes => auto-refreshed on next app open.
